records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17085
What was the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' system in 
Mithila?
To ensure 'Jati Shuddha' or caste purity.
17086
The term _____ refers to the detailed introduction of $32$ specific 
ancestors required to establish a Brahmin's lineage purity.
Udhedh
17087
Which historical ruler is credited with the formal initiation and 
inspiration of the Panji Prabandh in $1326$ AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
17088
In the context of Panji lineage, what is a 'Biji Purush'?
The earliest known ancestor from whom a specific lineage is traced.
17089
How many total 'Gotras' (clans) are identified among Maithil 
Brahmins in the Panji records?
$20$
17090
List the three main hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins 
established during the Panji division.
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
17091
What term is used for those who were not formally recorded in the 
Panji system but belong to the community?
Jaybar
17092
According to the marriage rules for 'Shrotriyas', how many 
generations must be avoided on the father's side?
$7$ generations.
17093
According to the marriage rules for 'Shrotriyas', how many 
generations must be avoided on the mother's side?
$5$ generations.
17094
Panjibaddha Brahmins, also known as lineage descendants, must 
avoid $5$ generations on the maternal side and _____ on the 
paternal side for marriage.
$6$ generations
17095
The two main Vedic divisions of Maithil Brahmins are the 
'Samavedi' (Chhandogya) and the 'Shukla Yajurvedi', known as 
_____.
Vajasaneyi
17096
What is the total number of 'Mula' (original roots) established in the 
Panji Prabandh?
$167$
17097
How many 'Mula Gram' (original villages) were designated as the 
primary roots out of the $167$ total?
$34$ (later $35$ with the addition of the 'Khama' branch).
17098
Define 'Tripravara' in the context of Maithil Brahmin lineages.
A lineage whose ancestors composed three specific hymns in the 
Rigveda.
17099
Define 'Panchapravara' in the context of Maithil Brahmin lineages.
A lineage whose ancestors composed five specific hymns in the 
Rigveda.
17100
Which three Gotras are specifically categorized as 'Panchapravara'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharve.
17101
Why is marriage prohibited between the 'Vatsa' and 'Savarna' 
Gotras despite being distinct?
Because they share the same ancient ancestors.
17102
Into how many broad regional groups are Indian Brahmins 
traditionally divided in the Puranic texts?
$10$ groups ($5$ Pancha Gauda and $5$ Pancha Dravida).
17103
Which regional group of the 'Pancha Gauda' division do the Maithil 
Brahmins belong to?
Maithil.
17104
What geographical feature acts as the boundary between the 
'Pancha Gauda' and 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmin groups?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
17105
The 'Vajasaneyi' (Yajurvedi) marriage ceremony can occur during 
the day or night, while the 'Chhandogya' (Samavedi) ceremony 
traditionally takes place after _____.
Sunset (after seeing the star Arundhati).
17106
Concept: Sapinda
Definition: Relatives who share the same pinda (ancestral 
body/offering), determining marriage eligibility and ritual impurity.
17107
According to Smriti texts like Manu and Yajnavalkya, a bride 
should not share the same _____ or Pravara as the groom.
Gotra
17108
Who was the 'Mahapandit' whose accidental marriage to a close 
relative (his paternal cousin's daughter) led to the formalisation of 
Panji records?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyay.
17109
What is the 'Siddhanta Patra' (also called 'Asvajana Patra')?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride 
and groom are not ineligible relatives.
17110
The official scholar appointed by the King to maintain and verify 
genealogical records is called a _____.
Panjikar
17111
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Mula Gram' represent?
The original village or homestead of a family's earliest recorded 
ancestor.
17112
How many 'Shrotriya' Panjis are currently maintained?
$32$
17113
How many 'Yogya' and 'Panjibaddha' Panjis are currently 
maintained?
$153$
17114
The term _____ refers to the specific calculation used to identify 
$16$ critical points in a lineage where a person is in the 'sixth' 
position.
Chhatva
17115
Under the 'Chhatva' rule, if any of the $16$ identified ancestors 
appear in the groom's paternal side within $6$ generations, is the 
marriage allowed?
No, it is prohibited.
17116
What is the 'Dushan Panji'?
A record detailing 'faults' or historical blemishes within specific 
lineages.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17117
Which script is traditionally used to write and preserve Panji 
records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
17118
The first official Panjikar for Maithil Brahmins appointed by 
Harisimhadeva was _____.
Gunakar Jha.
17119
Who was appointed as the first Panjikar for the 'Karna Kayasthas'?
Shankardatta.
17120
The Karna Kayasthas are generally associated with which single 
Gotra?
Kashyapa.
17121
According to the texts, during which period (months) should Panji 
books be dried in the sun to prevent damage?
When the Sun is in the Leo (Simha) rashi (approx. $16$ August to 
$15$ September).
17122
What is a 'Laukika' in the context of Panji classification?
The specific lineage branch or social rank within the Shrotriya 
category.
17123
The 'Shrotriya' Brahmins are sometimes referred to as 'Pubari' 
(Easterners), while others are called _____ (Westerners).
Pachhabari
17124
Which type of Panji record tracks changes in residence and family 
expansion over the last $500$ years?
Pan Panji.
17125
The historical figure _____ is recorded in the Panji as having the 
lineage title 'Kavishekhar' and serving in the Nepal court.
Jyotirishwar Thakur.
17126
Which famous saint's guru, Ishwar Puri, is linked to the 'Katih' 
Mula of the Maithil Brahmins in the records?
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.
17127
The ancient mathematician _____ is mentioned in the Panji lineage 
under the Mandaar Mula.
Aryabhata
17128
What is the primary Vedic branch followed by 'Chhandogya' 
Brahmins?
Samaveda (Kauthuma branch).
17129
What is the primary Vedic branch followed by 'Vajasaneyi' 
Brahmins?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina branch).
17130
The concept of 'Ayanati Shrestha' refers to which category of Mula? The first/highest category of original lineages (e.g. Khadaure, 
Khauale).
17131
How many ancestors are included in a standard 'Udhedh' for 
marriage verification?
$32$ ancestors (covering paternal and maternal lines).
17132
The scholar _____ is credited with establishing the rule that the 
$7^{th}$ generation on the paternal side is eligible for marriage.
Maharaja Mahesh Thakur.
17133
The term 'Svarnakarnaka' refers to what practice regarding Panji 
maintenance?
Digital imaging or copying of the Tirhuta script into modern 
Devanagari/digital formats.
17134
What is the primary objective of the 'Panji Prabandh' system in 
Mithila?
Maintaining 'Jati Shuddhata' (caste purity) and genealogical records.
17135
The Panji system was established to regulate lineages for which 
three social classes?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
17136
In the context of Panji, what does the term 'Udegh' signify?
The introduction of 32 ancestral lineages required to verify marriage 
eligibility.
17137
How many maternal generations must be avoided for a marriage to 
be considered valid under the Shrotriya rules?
Five generations.
17138
How many paternal generations must be avoided for a marriage to 
be considered valid under the Shrotriya rules?
Seven generations.
17139
Which specific ancestor count defines the 'Udegh' of a family?
A total of thirty-two ancestors.
17140
Name the four hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins 
established by the Panji system.
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddha, and Jayabar.
17141
What defines the 'Jayabar' category of Maithil Brahmins?
Those who were not formally registered in the Panji records.
17142
How many distinct 'Gotras' (clans) are identified in the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
Twenty.
17143
How many 'Moolas' (original ancestral villages) were identified 
during the system's inception?
One hundred and sixty-seven.
17144
Out of the 167 Moolas, how many are considered 'Main' or 
'Mukhya' Moolas?
Thirty-four or thirty-five.
17145
What is the difference between 'Tri-pravara' and 'Pancha-pravara'?
The number of ancestors (three or five) who were Rigvedic hymn 
composers.
17146
Which two major Vedic branches are followed by Maithil 
Brahmins?
Samaveda and Yajurveda.
17147
The Yajurvedi Maithil Brahmins follow which specific Shakha?
Madhyandina (Vajasaneyi).
17148
The Samavedi Maithil Brahmins follow which specific Shakha?
Kauthuma (Chhandogya).
17149
Which geographical boundary divides the 'Pancha Gauda' from the 
'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
17150
Maithil Brahmins belong to which broader regional grouping of 
North Indian Brahmins?
Pancha Gauda.
17151
Who was the Karnat dynasty king that formalised the Panji system?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
17152
In which year (AD) was the Mithila Panji system officially 
consolidated?
1326 AD (Saka 1248).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17153
What prompted the creation of the formal Panji system in the 14th 
century?
An accidental incestuous marriage involving Pandit Harinath 
Upadhyaya due to ignorance of lineage.
17154
What is 'Siddhanta' (or Siddhanta Patra) in Maithil marriage 
traditions?
A formal permit issued by a Panjikar certifying that the couple is 
not blood-related.
17155
Who is a 'Panjikar'?
A genealogical expert appointed to maintain records and verify 
marriage eligibility.
17156
Name the first Panjikar appointed for Maithil Brahmins.
Gunakar Jha.
17157
Name the first Panjikar appointed for the Karna Kayastha 
community.
Shankardatta.
17158
What is a 'Beeji Purusha'?
The earliest known progenitor or root ancestor of a specific lineage 
recorded in the Panji.
17159
Explain the concept of 'Moola Gram'.
The original village associated with the Beeji Purusha of a family 
line.
17160
According to the Panji system, what occurs if a person marries a 
'Svajana' (close relative)?
The offspring is traditionally considered 'Chandala' or socially 
excluded from Aryan status.
17161
Which philosopher's work, 'Tantra Vartika', provided early 7th-
century evidence for maintaining lineage records?
Kumarila Bhatta.
17162
The 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) were introduced during the 
reign of which king?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
17163
What does 'Dushana Panji' record?
Defects, violations of caste rules, or historical flaws in a family's 
lineage.
17164
In modern terms, what is the Panji system compared to by the 
authors?
Genome Mapping or Chromosome identification.
17165
How many 'Chhadvis' (ancestral checkpoints) are tracked to 
determine marriage eligibility?
Sixteen.
17166
A marriage is prohibited if any of the 16 'Chhadvis' of the bride 
appear in the _____ of the groom.
Paternal lineage (Pitripaksha).
17167
What is the rule regarding the Gotra of the bride and groom?
They must not belong to the same Gotra (Sagotra marriage is 
forbidden).
17168
Is a marriage between two people of the same Pravara permitted?
No, they must have distinct Pravaras.
17169
What is the prohibition regarding the bride and the groom's 
maternal uncle?
The groom cannot marry the daughter of his maternal uncle.
17170
How many Gotras are considered the most prominent or 
'Mahakulas' in Maithil society?
Seven (Shandilya, Vatsa, Savarna, Kashyapa, Parashara, 
Bharadwaja, and Katyayana).
17171
What is the significance of the 'Saka 1248' date in Mithila history?
It marks the formal registration and inception of the Panji records.
17172
Which hierarchical category is considered the highest in the Maithil 
Brahmin social order?
Shrotriya.
17173
The term 'Laukika' refers to which type of Panji specifically?
The Shrotriya Panji.
17174
Under which conditions can the Panji books be dried to ensure 
preservation?
When the sun is in the Leo (Simha) constellation (approx. August-
September).
17175
What three things must Panji books be protected from according to 
traditional verses?
Water (Jala), Oil (Taila), and loose binding (Shithila Bandhan).
17176
How many hierarchical 'Chauwalas' (classes) exist within the 
Shrotriya Panji?
Eight.
17177
What is the 'Asvajana Patra'?
A document proving that the bride and groom are not related by 
blood within prohibited degrees.
17178
Which Panji type tracks descendants and branch-wise expansions 
of a family?
Shakha Panji.
17179
What are 'Aandhi' Moolas?
Main ancestral lineages from which various sub-branches 
(Shakhas) emerge.
17180
Followers of the Kauthuma Shakha are specifically known as _____. Chhandoga.
17181
Followers of the Madhyandina Shakha are specifically known as 
_____.
Vajasaneyi.
17182
What is the relationship between the 'Vatsa' and 'Savarna' Gotras 
regarding marriage?
Marriage between them is forbidden because they share the same 
ancestors.
17183
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Mandi' village and has 74 sub-
branches?
Kashyapa.
17184
Which category of Brahmins is referred to as 'Pachhwari-paat'?
The non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddha).
17185
The 'Adho-gami' nature of Panji refers to what social phenomenon?
The decline in status resulting from marriage into lower hierarchical 
ranks.
17186
Who was the 'Beeji Purusha' of the Simhashram Moola according 
to the text?
Mahamahopadhyaya Halayudha.
17187
In Karna Kayasthas, how many Gotras are typically recognised?
One (Kashyapa).
17188
What does 'Sapiá¹‡á¸ a' refer to in the context of marriage laws?
Sharing the same body particles or having common ancestors 
within defined generations.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17189
Who was the first Kshatriya Panjikar appointed by King 
Harisimhadeva?
Vijayadatta.
17190
What distinguishes 'Tri-pravara' Gotras from 'Panch-pravara' Gotras? Tri-pravara Gotras cite three sages in their ritual invocation; Panch-
pravara cite five.
17191
List three Gotras that fall under the 'Panch-pravara' category.
Vatsa, Savarna, and Garga.
17192
Name the first 'Chhadvi' (ancestor) checked for a bride's eligibility.
The paternal great-great-grandfather (Vriddha Prapitamaha).
17193
What role does the 'Dushana Panji' play in historical research?
It provides evidence of historical social anomalies, such as inter-
caste marriages or widow remarriages.
17194
Which kingâ€™s assembly appointed Gunakar Jha as the head of 
Panji Prabandh?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
17195
What is the 'Prathama Chhadvi' in the verification process?
The bride's paternal great-great-grandfather.
17196
Marriage with a 'Matama-gotra' (mother's gotra) is prohibited if the 
groom is within which generation?
Within seven generations on the father's side and five on the 
mother's side.
17197
What does the text claim is the 'Pranadhara' (life force) of the 
Tirhuta script?
The Panji Prabandh records.
17198
In the Panji terminology, what is a 'Kula'?
A lineage or family group sharing common heritage.
17199
Why is the Maithil Panji system considered 'unparalleled' by the 
authors?
It contains continuous genealogical records for over 1000 years 
across an entire community.
17200
Who was the author of the 'Smritisara', whose marriage mistake led 
to the Panji system?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya.
17201
What is 'Pancha Gauda'?
The five regional Brahmin groups of North India: Saraswat, 
Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Utkal.
17202
What is 'Pancha Dravida'?
The five regional Brahmin groups of South India: Karnataka, 
Gurjara, Tailanga, Dravida, and Maharashtra.
17203
Which Gotra includes sages Shandilya, Asita, and Devala in its 
Pravara?
Shandilya.
17204
The 'Vajasaneyi' marriage ceremony can occur at what time?
During the day or night, as there is no specific restriction mentioned 
compared to Chhandoga.
17205
The 'Chhandoga' marriage ceremony must occur after which event?
After sunset, following the sighting of the star Arundhati.
17206
What does the 'Moola Panji' specifically track?
The original ancestral identity and village of a family.
17207
The 'Genome Mapping' of Mithila lineages described in the text 
spans from _____ AD to 2009 AD.
450
17208
Term: Udhredh
Definition: The formal introduction or record of seven generations 
of ancestors used to determine marriage eligibility.
17209
In the Panji system, how many total ancestors (kula) are 
traditionally identified in a full 'udhredh' introduction?
32
17210
Name the three main hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins 
created through the Panji system.
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
17211
What term is used for those who were not formally recorded in the 
Panji system?
Jaywar
17212
According to traditional Shrotriya rules, how many generations 
must be avoided for marriage on the maternal side?
Five generations.
17213
According to traditional Shrotriya rules, how many generations 
must be avoided for marriage on the paternal side?
Seven generations.
17214
How many 'Gotras' are formally recognised within the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
20
17215
How many 'Mula Grams' (original villages) were initially identified 
during the Panji classification?
167
17216
Out of the 167 original villages, how many are considered the 'Main 
Mulas' (primary roots)?
34 (later expanded to 35).
17217
Which sage-based lineages belong to the 'Panch-pravara' (five-
ancestor) category?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharva.
17218
Define the term 'Tri-pravara' in the context of Maithil Gotras.
Lineages whose three ancestors were composers of Vedic hymns 
(suktas).
17219
Why is marriage prohibited between the Vatsa and Savarna Gotras 
despite being separate names?
Because they share the same common ancestors (identical Pravaras).
17220
According to the 'Pancha Gauda' classification, which five Brahmin 
groups reside north of the Vindhya mountains?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithila, and Saraswata.
17221
Which historical region's capital was the original home of the 
Maithil Brahmins?
Videha
17222
Maithil Brahmins follow which specific branch (shakha) of the 
Yajurveda?
Madhyandina
17223
What name is given to Maithil Brahmins who follow the Kauthuma 
branch of the Samaveda?
Chandoga
17224
Maithil Brahmins who follow the Madhyandina branch of the 
Yajurveda are known as _____.
Vajasaneyi

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17225
How does the 'Uttara Vivaha' of Samavedic (Chandoga) Brahmins 
differ from Yajurvedic Brahmins?
It must take place after sunset following the viewing of the 
Arundhati star.
17226
The first reference to 'Samuha Lekhya' (collective lineage records) 
is found in which philosopher's work?
Kumarila Bhatta (in the Tantravarttika).
17227
Which king of the Karnata dynasty is credited with the formalised 
'Panji Prabandh' in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva
17228
What was the specific year in the Shaka calendar when the Panji 
system was established?
Shaka 1248
17229
Who was the scholar appointed by Maharaja Harisimhadeva to 
organise the Maithil Brahmin Panji?
Saduphadhyaya Gunakar Jha
17230
The certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride and 
groom are not related (non-sapinda) is called the _____.
Siddhanta Patra (or Asvajanapa Patra)
17231
The scholar appointed for the Panji management of Karna 
Kayasthas was _____.
Shankar Datta
17232
The scholar appointed for the Panji management of the Kshatriya 
community was _____.
Vijay Datta
17233
Under whose reign was the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) 
initiated, leading to modern social divisions?
Maharaja Madhav Singh
17234
How many Gotras are identified for Karna Kayasthas in the Panji 
system?
One (Kashyap).
17235
In the preservation of Panji manuscripts, during which month 
should the books be dried in the sun?
When the Sun is in the zodiac of Leo (approx. 16th August to 15th 
September).
17236
Marriage is prohibited if either the bride or groom falls into the 
_____ position (Chhatth) of the other's lineage.
Sixth
17237
M.M. Maharaja Mahesh Thakur ruled that the _____ generation of 
the paternal side is acceptable for marriage.
Seventh
17238
The primary language and script used for the original Panji records 
is _____.
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar)
17239
Concept: Laukik
Definition: The social status or 'honour' level recorded within the 
Shrotriya Panji branches.
17240
In the calculation of marriage eligibility, how many specific 
positions (Chhatth) are verified in the bride's lineage?
16 positions.
17241
Which Gotra does the scholar Gunakar Jha (the first Brahmin 
Panjikar) belong to?
Sandilya (specifically the Mohi-ji branch).
17242
What is the consequence of marrying a 'Svajana' (prohibited 
relative) according to the Smritis mentioned in the text?
The offspring are considered 'Chandala' and the parents are 
ostracised from the Arya dignity.
17243
The term 'Pubaripaha' refers to which category of Maithil Brahmins? Shrotriya Brahmins.
17244
The term 'Pachhbaripaha' refers to which category of Maithil 
Brahmins?
Jaywar Brahmins (non-Shrotriya).
17245
Which type of Panji documents the 'Sapinda' (blood relation) details 
and ancestral names?
Udhredh Panji
17246
The text lists _____ as a famous historical figure whose guru, 
Iban/Pratitidatta, is recorded in the Panji.
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
17247
Term: Pancha Dravida
Definition: The five Brahmin groups residing south of the Vindhya 
mountains (Karnat, Gurjar, Tailang, Dravid, and Maharashtra).
17248
According to the text, a student of Panji Shastra must avoid _____ 
when handling manuscripts to prevent decay.
Water, oil, and loose binding.
17249
The scholar _____ is mentioned as having identified 4,000 Gotras 
in his historical research.
M.P. Chichalal Rao
17250
Which Gotra is associated with the Pravaras: Kashyap, Avatsar, and 
Naidhruva?
Kashyap
17251
What is the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' in the Mithila 
region?
To maintain caste purity and determine marriage eligibility for 
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
17252
In what year was the formal Panji system established under the 
patronage of King Harisimhadeva?
1326 AD (Saka 1248).
17253
Who was the Maithil Brahmin appointed by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Brahmin Panji records?
Gunakar Jha.
17254
The term 'Uteá¸ h' refers to the introduction and lineage of how 
many ancestors required for marriage verification?
Thirty-two ancestors (Battiis Kul).
17255
Identify the three main social tiers of Maithil Brahmins established 
during the Panji management.
Srotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
17256
What is the common name for Brahmins who were not formally 
recorded in the Panji system?
Jaebaar (or Jayabaar).
17257
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided for marriage on the father's side?
Seven generations.
17258
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided for marriage on the mother's side?
Five generations.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17259
The Panji records identify a total of how many distinct 'Gotras' 
(clans) among Maithil Brahmins?
Twenty.
17260
How many 'MÅ«l Grams' (original villages) were initially identified 
in the Panji system?
One hundred and sixty-seven.
17261
Out of the 167 original villages, how many were designated as the 
'Main MÅ«ls' (Mukhya MÅ«l)?
Thirty-four (later thirty-five including the Khanam branch of 
Phandah).
17262
The term 'Beeji Purush' refers to which individual in a lineage?
The earliest known ancestor or the progenitor of a specific 
clan/branch.
17263
What defines the 'Tri-Pravara' category in the Panji system?
Clans whose three ancestors were composers of Rigvedic hymns.
17264
Which three Maithil Brahmin Gotras are classified as 'Panch-
Pravara'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaj (though the text lists 
Bhargava/Vatsa/Savarna variants).
17265
Name the two Vedic branches followed by Maithil Brahmins.
Yajurvedic (Madhyandini branch) and Samavedic (Kauthum 
branch).
17266
What is the title given to Maithil Brahmins who follow the 
Samavedic Kauthum branch?
Chhandogya.
17267
What is the title given to Maithil Brahmins who follow the 
Yajurvedic Madhyandini branch?
Vajasaneyi.
17268
Explain the significance of the document known as 'Siddhanta 
Patra' (or Ashvajan Patra).
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride 
and groom are not related within forbidden degrees.
17269
The 'Pancha Gauda' classification of Brahmins includes which five 
regional groups?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
17270
The 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins are geographically located in which 
direction relative to the Vindhyachal mountains?
South.
17271
Why is marriage traditionally forbidden between the Savarna and 
Vatsa Gotras in the Panji system?
Because they share the same ancestors (lineage) despite having 
different clan names.
17272
What is the specific duty of a 'Panjikar'?
To maintain genealogical records and verify the legality of 
marriages based on lineage.
17273
In the context of Panji, what does the term 'Chhadvith' (or Chhad-
vrit) represent?
The sixteen specific forbidden relations or 'checkpoints' in the 
lineage of the bride and groom.
17274
What is the first 'Chhadvith' rule regarding the bride's lineage?
The relationship to the bride's paternal great-great-grandfather.
17275
Identify the 'Main MÅ«l' associated with the Sandilya Gotra that 
has many branches.
Digho (or Dighve).
17276
Which scholar's 7th-century work 'Tantra Varttika' mentions the 
'Samuh Lekhya' as a precursor to the Panji system?
Kumarila Bhatta.
17277
The 'Dushana Panji' records what specific type of information?
Flaws or incidents of 'impurity' within a lineage to prevent their 
recurrence.
17278
What is the difference between marriage rituals for Chhandogya 
and Vajasaneyi Brahmins regarding the 'Tara' (stars)?
Chhandogya marriages involve viewing the Arundhati star after 
sunset, while Vajasaneyi marriages have no such requirement.
17279
The term 'MÅ«l' in Panji theory primarily designates which two 
identifiers?
Progenitor (Beeji Purush) and Original Village (MÅ«l Gram).
17280
How many 'Sakha Panjis' (branch records) were initiated during the 
reign of Maharaja Madhav Singh?
The text notes they began during his reign to manage increasing 
social divisions and hierarchies.
17281
Which Gotra is the only one found among the Karna Kayasthas 
according to the source?
Kashyap.
17282
What was the 'Siddhanta' requirement introduced to ensure 
scriptural compliance in marriages?
No marriage is considered valid unless verified and approved by an 
appointed Panjikar.
17283
The classification 'Pachhwari Paat' refers to which group of Maithil 
Brahmins?
The non-Srotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddh).
17284
How many categories of marriage records (Panjis) are mentioned, 
such as MÅ«l, Sakha, and Gotra?
Seven (MÅ«l, Sakha, Gotra, Patra, Dushana, Uteá¸ h, and Ashvajan-
Patra).
17285
What unique genealogical document is used to track 'Sapinda' 
(blood relation) limits?
Uteá¸ h Panji.
17286
According to the text, what happened to the Panji system for the 
Kshatriya community over time?
It became extinct/obsolete, leading them to rely only on avoiding 
identical village names for marriages.
17287
Identify the four sacraments (Samskaras) involved in a Chhandogya 
Upanayana.
Namkaran, Chudakaran, Upanayana, and Samavartan.
17288
Which Brahmin subgroup was traditionally called 'Pubari Paat'?
Srotriya Brahmins.
17289
What is the recommended method for preserving Panji manuscripts 
according to the traditional verse provided?
Protecting them from water, oil, and loose binding (Jalaat Rakshe, 
Tailaat Rakshe).
17290
When is the best time to dry Panji manuscripts to prevent damage, 
according to the text?
When the Sun is in the Leo (Simha) constellation (approx. mid-
August to mid-September).
17291
Who was the first Panjikar appointed for the Karna Kayastha 
community?
Shankardatta.
17292
Who was the first Panjikar appointed for the Kshatriya community? Vijaydatta.
17293
Concept: Pravara
Definition: The list of sage-ancestors associated with a Gotra who 
were composers of Vedic hymns.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17294
The Panji system is often compared to which modern scientific 
field in the source title?
Genome Mapping.
17295
Which city was the capital of the ancient Videha dynasty, the 
cultural heart of Mithila?
Mithila (Janakpur).
17296
What determines if a Chhadvith relationship allows marriage?
A relation is forbidden if the ancestor appears in the groom's 
paternal side within 6 degrees, but permitted if found on the 
maternal side.
17297
Under what condition is a child considered 'Chandal' in traditional 
Smriti laws mentioned in the text?
When born from a marriage between 'Svajana' (forbidden blood 
relatives).
17298
Identify the Gotra associated with the sage 'Atri' and 'Sankriti' in the 
Tri-Pravara list.
Vashistha.
17299
Which Gotra includes the sages 'Vatsa', 'Asita', and 'Devala'?
Sandilya.
17300
Name one 'Ati-Srestha' (first-tier) MÅ«l mentioned in the text.
Kharaure, Khauware, or Budhabaware (among others).
17301
What is the significance of the 35th MÅ«l, 'Khanam'?
It is a branch of the Phandah MÅ«l that was elevated to high status 
in later periods.
17302
How does the Panji record describe the status of a 'Jaebari' 
Brahmin?
A Brahmin whose family lineage was not formally recorded in the 
organized Panji system.
17303
The text lists _____ as a key reason for the regional diversification 
of Brahmins in ancient India.
Climate, language, diet, and dress (Geographical influences).
17304
The title 'Vidyapati Thakur' appears in the Panji records; how many 
generations of his lineage are said to be documented?
Lineage starting from seven generations before him down to his 
modern descendants.
17305
What historical script is primarily used to write Panji records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
17306
According to the Panji calculations, if an ancestor is found in the 
'six' (Chhadvith) for a bride, can she marry a groom if that ancestor 
is in his maternal side?
Yes, it is permissible.
17307
Which ancestor is considered the 'Progenitor' for the Mandar village 
branch of the Kashyap Gotra?
Tri-Nayan-Bhatta.
17308
The 'Laukik' system is specifically associated with which tier of 
Brahmins?
Srotriya.
17309
How many 'Chowalas' (categories) exist within the Srotriya Panji?
Eight.
17310
What is the total number of Srotriya Panjis listed in the text?
Thirty-two.
17311
What is the total number of Yogya and Panjibaddh Panjis listed in 
the text?
One hundred and fifty-three.
17312
Name the Gotra that includes the sages 'Shakti', 'Vashistha', and 
'Parashara'.
Parashara.
17313
Which Gotra is associated with the sages 'Katyayana', 'Vishnu', and 
'Angirasa'?
Katyayana.
17314
Who was the 'Beeji Purush' of the Simhashram MÅ«l mentioned as 
a contemporary of King Lakshman Sen?
Halayudha.
17315
What is the role of 'Dushana' in a lineage, as per the Panji records?
It marks a historical fault (e.g., inter-caste marriage or social exile) 
to allow future generations to account for it.
17316
According to the text, Maharaja Mahesh Thakur changed the 
father's side marriage restriction to which generation?
The 7th generation (making the 7th generation eligible for marriage).
17317
What was the purpose of creating 'Adhikaar Maalas' (eligibility 
lists) for young girls 5-10 years before marriage?
To pre-identify eligible grooms and simplify the final 'Siddhanta' 
process.
17318
Which Gotra corresponds to the sages 'Mudgala', 'Angirasa', and 
'BhÄrmyashva'?
Mudgala.
17319
Identify the two types of Upanayana ceremonies mentioned for 
Vajasaneyi Brahmins.
Upanayana and VedÄ rambha.
17320
What was the traditional writing surface for Panji records before 
paper?
Palm leaves (Taal-Patra).
17321
How does the Panji define a 'Main MÅ«l' versus a 'MÅ«l Gram'?
A Main MÅ«l is the ancestral source village, while MÅ«l Gram can 
refer to the village of residence during the formal establishment of 
the Panji.
17322
Which Gotra is linked to the sages 'Vishnuvriddha', 'Kandapuccha', 
and 'Trasadasyu'?
Vishnuvriddha.
17323
The phrase 'Maatritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami' refers to what 
rule?
Avoiding five generations on the mother's side and seven on the 
father's side for marriage.
17324
According to the text, why did Srotriya Brahmins eventually get 
subdivided into 'Pahari' and other tiers?
Due to marriages into lower-tier families leading to a gradual 
decline in rank (Adhogami).
17325
The term 'Beeji' effectively functions as what in a genealogical tree? The root or starting point of that specific family's documented 
history.
17326
What historical time period is covered by the Mithila Panji 
Prabandh genealogy records?
The records span from 450 AD to 2009 AD.
17327
Which ruler is credited with the formal initiation of the Panji 
Prabandh in Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva of the Karnat dynasty initiated the system.
17328
In what year of the Christian calendar was the Panji system 
officially established?
It was established in 1326 AD.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17329
The official establishment of the Panji system corresponds to which 
year in the Saka calendar?
It corresponds to Saka 1248.
17330
What was the primary social objective for the creation of the Panji 
Prabandh?
The primary objective was to ensure caste purity and regulate 
marriage eligibility.
17331
According to the Panji system, how many ancestral lineages 
comprise a full 'Uderh' introduction?
A full introduction comprises 32 ancestral lineages.
17332
On the paternal side, marriage is prohibited within how many 
generations?
Marriage is prohibited within seven generations on the paternal side.
17333
On the maternal side, marriage is prohibited within how many 
generations?
Marriage is prohibited within five generations on the maternal side.
17334
How many total Gotras are recognised among the Maithil Brahmins? There are 20 recognised Gotras.
17335
What is the collective name for the five northern Brahmin groups, 
including the Maithils?
They are known as the Pancha Gauda.
17336
What is the collective name for the five southern Brahmin groups?
They are known as the Pancha Dravida.
17337
Which mountain range serves as the geographical boundary 
between the Pancha Gauda and Pancha Dravida?
The Vindhyachal Mountains serve as the boundary.
17338
What defines a 'Tri-pravara' lineage in the Maithil Brahmin 
community?
It is a lineage where three ancestors are credited with composing 
Vedic hymns.
17339
What defines a 'Panch-pravara' lineage?
It is a lineage where five ancestors are credited with composing 
Vedic hymns.
17340
Followers of the Madhyandina branch of the Shukla Yajurveda are 
referred to as _____.
Vajasaneyi
17341
Followers of the Kauthuma branch of the Samaveda are referred to 
as _____.
Chandoga
17342
What is the 'Siddhanta Patra' in the context of Mithila marriages?
It is a certificate issued by a Panjikar verifying marriage eligibility.
17343
What is the common name for the genealogist who maintains 
family records and verifies eligibility?
The genealogist is known as a Panjikar.
17344
Which Brahmin scholar was first entrusted with the Panji 
management by King Harisimhadeva?
Pandit Gunakar Jha was the first to be entrusted.
17345
In Panji terminology, what does the term 'Biji Purush' represent?
It represents the founding ancestor of a specific lineage or 'Mool'.
17346
How many 'Mools' (original villages/lineages) were initially 
identified for the Brahmin community?
There were 167 original Mools identified.
17347
The 'Shrotriya' classification refers to which group of Maithil 
Brahmins?
It refers to the highest rank within the caste hierarchy.
17348
Marriage alliances are prohibited between the Vatsa and Savarna 
Gotras because they share common _____.
Ancestors (Pravara)
17349
Which historical incident involving Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya led 
to the formalisation of the Panji system?
He unknowingly married a close relative due to a lack of 
genealogical records.
17350
What is 'Dushan Panji'?
It is a record of faults or social transgressions within a lineage.
17351
Who introduced the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) during the later 
period?
Maharaja Madhav Singh introduced the Shakha Panji.
17352
What are the four essential sacraments for a Chandoga Brahmin?
They are Namakarana, Chudakarana, Upanayana, and Samavartana.
17353
Identify the primary script historically used to record the Panji 
manuscripts.
The Tirhuta (Mithilakshar) script was used.
17354
The term 'Moolgram' in the Panji system refers to the original 
_____ of a family.
Village
17355
Which Gotra is universally applied to the Karna Kayastha 
community in Mithila?
The Kashyap Gotra is applied to the entire community.
17356
What are the three main classes of Maithil Brahmins created during 
the Panji organisation?
The classes are Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
17357
What is 'Asvajana Patra'?
It is another name for the certificate of non-relationship required for 
marriage.
17358
How many hymn-composing ancestors are listed for the Sandilya 
Gotra?
There are three ancestors: Sandilya, Asit, and Deval.
17359
Which ancestors are shared by the Vatsa and Savarna Gotras?
They share Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and 
Apnuvan.
17360
What does 'Samuh Lekhya' refer to in the context of 7th-century 
genealogy?
It refers to documented collective family introductions mentioned 
by Kumarila Bhatta.
17361
The high-ranking 'Ayanthi Shrestha' category consists of how many 
primary Mools?
It consists of 13 primary Mools.
17362
What is 'Chhadwith' in the Panji marriage eligibility rules?
It refers to six ancestral positions used to determine prohibited 
relationships.
17363
Why must Panji books be dried when the sun enters the zodiac of 
Leo (Simha)?
It is the time of most intense heat, which helps preserve the palm 
leaves from moisture.
17364
Which 7th-century text is cited as an early reference for caste purity 
through lineage records?
The 'Tantra Vartika' by Kumarila Bhatta is cited.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17365
What are the three ancestors associated with the Parashar Gotra?
They are Shakti, Vashistha, and Parashar.
17366
According to the source, what is the role of 'Biji Purush' in 
identifying a lineage?
He is the oldest known ancestor from whom the lineage is traced.
17367
What are the three ancestors associated with the Bharadwaj Gotra?
They are Bharadwaj, Angiras, and Barhaspatya.
17368
In the context of Panji records, what does the 'Laukik' status refer 
to?
It refers to the social and genealogical standing of a Shrotriya 
family.
17369
What is the '1st Chhadwith' for a girl in the eligibility check?
It is her paternal great-grandfather's father.
17370
How many grades or 'Chauras' are there in the Shrotriya 
classification?
There are eight grades.
17371
What happened to the Panji system of the Kshatriyas over time?
It eventually became extinct and is no longer actively maintained.
17372
Which scholar's lineage is traced back to M.M. Halayudha and Biji 
Purush Trivikram Bhatta?
The scholar is Vidyanand Jha Mohiji.
17373
Who was the first Panjikar appointed for the Kayastha community?
Shankardutta was the first appointee.
17374
The 'Uttar Vivaha' ceremony, performed after seeing the star 
Arundhati, is specific to which Veda followers?
It is specific to the followers of the Samaveda (Chandogas).
17375
In Panji records, 'Dighwe' and 'Belonche' are examples of which 
Mool category?
They are examples of Madhyam Mools.
17376
What is the maximum number of Gotras listed in the Maithil 
Brahmin Panji?
The maximum number is 20.
17377
Name the three ancestors of the Kashyap Gotra Pravara.
They are Kashyap, Avatsara, and Naidhruva.
17378
The classification 'Panjibaddh' refers to those whose names were 
included in the _____.
Formalised genealogical records (Panji)
17379
Which ancestors define the Katyayan Gotra Pravara?
They are Katyayan, Vishnu, and Angiras.
17380
Which ancestors define the Gautam Gotra Pravara?
They are Angiras, Vashistha, and Barhaspatya.
17381
Identify the founding ancestor (Biji Purush) of the Mandar Mool of 
Kashyap Gotra.
Trivikram Bhatta is the founding ancestor.
17382
What is the 'Dushan' associated with a lineage?
It refers to genealogical 'impurities' or errors in the family line.
17383
The 'Pachhwari' Brahmins are those associated with the _____ of 
Mithila.
Western region
17384
Which text defines 'Svapinda' as those who are excluded from 
marriage due to shared ancestry?
The Smritis, specifically Manu and Yajnavalkya, define this.
17385
What does the term 'Adhahgami' imply about the status of a Panji?
It implies a downward movement or decline in lineage rank over 
time.
17386
Who was the teacher of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu mentioned in the 
Panji records?
Ramapati Upadhyaya was his teacher.
17387
To which Mool did the teacher of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu belong?
He belonged to the Kariyan Mool of the Vatsa Gotra.
17388
The poet Vidyapati Thakur's genealogy is recorded under which 
Panji branch?
It is recorded under the Bisphi branch of the Kashyap Gotra.
17389
What is the role of the 'Biji Purush' in determining marriage 
eligibility?
The Biji Purush helps identify the root lineage to check for common 
ancestry.
17390
How many ancestors are included in the 'Uderh' of the groom's 
paternal side for a marriage check?
There are six ancestors included.
17391
How many ancestors are included in the 'Uderh' of the groom's 
maternal side for a marriage check?
There are five ancestors included.
17392
The term 'Mool' identifies the lineage's connection to an original 
_____.
Village or habitat
17393
Which manuscript preservation technique is mentioned as being 
taught to Panji students?
The protection of books from water, oil, and loose binding.
17394
What is the '16th Chhadwith' for a girl in the Panji system?
It is her maternal grandmother's maternal grandfather.
17395
Which Gotra includes the ancestors Mudgala, Angiras, and 
Barhaspatya?
The Mudgala Gotra includes these ancestors.
17396
Who is the '5th Chhadwith' for a girl in the eligibility check?
It is her paternal grandmother's paternal great-grandfather.
17397
What is the '8th Chhadwith' for a girl?
It is her paternal grandmother's maternal grandfather.
17398
The term 'Ansh' refers to a _____ within a larger Mool.
Sub-branch or specific family line
17399
In Mithila genealogy, what is the 'Biji' of a Mool?
It is the source ancestor or the first person to settle in a specific 
Moolgram.
17400
How does the Shakha Panji differ from the Mool Panji?
The Shakha Panji is a more detailed branch record introduced later 
to track social status.
17401
Which classification of Maithil Brahmins is traditionally associated 
with the highest Vedic scholarship?
The Shrotriya classification is associated with this.
17402
The 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins reside south of which mountain 
range?
They reside south of the Vindhyachal Mountains.
17403
What is the penalty mentioned for marrying a 'Svajana' (prohibited 
relative) in ancient times?
The couple was socially boycotted and their children were 
considered 'Chandala'.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17404
Identify the primary source for the 20 Gotras listed in the Mithila 
Panji.
The primary sources are the Vedic lineages of the seven great sages 
(Saptarishi).
17405
During which century was the formalisation of the Panji system 
initiated under King Harisimhadeva?
The 14th century (specifically 1326 AD or Shaka 1248).
17406
In the context of Panji genealogy, what does the term 'Utegh' 
represent?
A detailed introduction or list of 32 ancestral families (Mools) used 
to verify marriage eligibility.
17407
According to 'Panji Theory', how many generations must be 
avoided for marriage on the paternal side for a Srotriya Brahmin?
Seven generations.
17408
According to the Panji system, how many generations must be 
avoided for marriage on the maternal side?
Five generations.
17409
The Maithil Brahmin community is divided into how many 'Gotras' 
according to the source material?
20 Gotras.
17410
What are the three main social categories of Maithil Brahmins 
established by the Panji Prabandh?
Srotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
17411
What is the historical significance of the 'Shaka 1248' date in 
Mithila's history?
It marks the formal establishment of the Panji records to prevent 
prohibited marriages.
17412
Who was the 'Beji Purush' in the context of Mithila's genealogical 
records?
The earliest known or founding ancestor of a specific lineage or 
clan.
17413
Which two Vedas are primarily followed by Maithil Brahmins?
The Samaveda (Chandogya) and the Shukla Yajurveda 
(Vajasaneyi).
17414
What is the difference between 'Tri-Pravara' and 'Panch-Pravara' in 
Brahmin lineages?
Tri-Pravara lineages have three sage ancestors who composed 
Rigvedic hymns, while Panch-Pravara have five.
17415
Which specific Brahmin Gotras are categorised as 'Panch-Pravara'?
Vatsa, Saavarna, and Bharadwaj.
17416
What is a 'Moolgram' in the Panji system?
The original village or ancestral seat of a particular lineage.
17417
According to the Panji system, why can marriages not occur 
between the Vatsa and Saavarna Gotras?
Because they share the same set of five primary sage ancestors 
(Pravaras).
17418
The Panji system identifies how many 'main' Mools (lineages) for 
Maithil Brahmins?
35 (originally 34, plus the Khanam branch of the Phandah Mool).
17419
Which king of the Karnata dynasty is credited with starting the 
Panji Prabandh?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
17420
Who was appointed as the first registrar (Panjikar) for Maithil 
Brahmins by King Harisimhadeva?
Gunanakar Jha.
17421
Who was the first Panjikar appointed specifically for the Karna 
Kayasthas?
Shankardatta.
17422
What is the role of a 'Panjikar' in Mithila society?
To maintain genealogical books and verify the eligibility of couples 
before marriage.
17423
In Maithil Brahmin classification, who are the 'Jaywars'?
Those who are not officially recorded in the Panji books or fall 
outside the top three categories.
17424
What specific incident involving Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya led to 
the formalisation of the Panji records?
He inadvertently married a close relative (his cousin's daughter) due 
to lack of recorded genealogical data.
17425
What are the two major geographical divisions of Brahmins in India 
according to the Puranas?
Pancha Gauda (Northern) and Pancha Dravida (Southern).
17426
Into which broader group do Maithil Brahmins fall within the ten-
fold Puranic classification?
The Pancha Gauda group.
17427
In the Panji system, what does 'Laukik' refer to?
The specific lineage records or classifications used primarily for the 
Srotriya category.
17428
How many Srotriya Panji records are currently identified in the 
system?
32 Panjis.
17429
How many Panji records exist for the Yogya and Panjibaddh 
categories combined?
153 Panjis.
17430
What is 'Dooshan Panji' used for?
To record any flaws, social downgrades, or violations of tradition 
within a specific lineage.
17431
What is 'Sakha Panji' and who introduced it?
It is a branch-specific genealogical record introduced during the 
time of Maharaja Madhav Singh.
17432
In the Maithil Panji system, what is the 'Chhatth' (Sixth) rule?
A calculation identifying 16 specific ancestors (8 paternal, 8 
maternal) to ensure a groom is not within the prohibited 6th degree 
of the bride.
17433
Under what condition can a 'Chhatth' ancestor appear in the groom's 
lineage without prohibiting the marriage?
If the ancestor appears only on the maternal side of the groom's 
lineage (as opposed to the paternal side).
17434
What are the terms 'Pubaripaar' and 'Pachbaripaar' used to 
distinguish?
The eastern (Srotriya) and western (remaining Brahmin) divisions 
of Maithil Brahmins.
17435
What is the 'Utegh Panji' specifically designed to reveal?
The 'Saprinda' relationships (shared bloodline) of a person across 
multiple generations.
17436
According to the text, what are the three things from which Panji 
books must be protected?
Water, oil, and loose bindings.
17437
When is the recommended time of year to dry and preserve Panji 
books?
When the Sun is in the Leo (Simha) constellation (roughly 16 
August to 15 September).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17438
What is the 'Mool Panji'?
The original, consolidated genealogical record of the different 
Brahmin clans.
17439
Why is the Panji Prabandh referred to as 'Genome Mapping' in the 
source title?
Because it systematically tracks genetic lineage and ancestry over 
1,500 years to prevent inbreeding.
17440
Which Maithil poetâ€™s lineage is extensively documented in the 
Panji records?
Mahakavi Vidyapati Thakur.
17441
According to the records, which famous Indian mathematician's 
lineage is traced to the Mandar Mool?
Aryabhata.
17442
Which religious reformer's teacher, Ishwar Puri, is identified 
through the Panji records?
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.
17443
What are the four types of religious ceremonies (Samskaras) 
specifically different for Chandogya Brahmins?
Namkarana, Chudakarana, Upayana, and Samavartana.
17444
In the Vajasaneyi marriage tradition, at what time can the ceremony 
take place?
It can occur at any time during the day or night (unlike Chandogya, 
which has specific timing).
17445
Term: Saprinda
Definition: A relative connected by the same body or 'pinda' 
(ancestral offering), within prohibited degrees for marriage.
17446
How many Rajput clans are mentioned in the 'Varna Ratnakara' in 
relation to the Panji system?
72 clans (though 64 are described in detail).
17447
What is the significance of the year 2000 AD in the context of the 
source material?
It marked the beginning of digitising, transcribing, and editing the 
ancient Tirhuta Panji records into Devanagari.
17448
Who are the 'Ayanishrestha' Mools?
The first or highest category of lineages, including Mools like 
Khardore, Khauare, and Sarisab.
17449
The Panji system forbids marrying the child of which maternal 
relative?
The maternal uncle (mother's brother).
17450
What does the 'Aswajanyapan Panji' specifically track?
The genealogy of families that have lost their original social status 
or moved to different regions.
17451
How many generations of maternal ancestors are traditionally 
tracked in a standard Panji 'Utegh'?
Five generations.
17452
According to the source material, how many primary types of Panji 
(genealogical records) exist in Mithila?
Seven
17453
Which specific type of Panji record is dedicated to documenting 
'depletion' or flaws within a family lineage?

$1248$ Shaka (1326 AD)

$1019$

Gangesha Upadhyaya

Vardhamana

Jagadguru

Vacaspati Mishra II (Nutan Vachaspati)

Chhadan

He was born five years after the passing of his father.

It is one of the seven types of Panji used in the genealogical system

What is the primary historical purpose of the 'Panji Prabandha'

1326 AD

Genome Mapping
17652
The Karnata king Nanyadeva is mentioned in the text as an ancestor 
of which famous king?
Harisimhadeva
17653
The text mentions that inter-caste marriages or marriages with 
'unknown lineages' are recorded in which supplementary Panji?
Dushan Panji
17654
What was the original source of genealogical tracking before the 
written Panji system was established?
Oral tradition and personal records kept by families.
17655
According to the Panji theory, why did the Brahmin community 
divide into regional groups like Utkala or Maithil?
Due to differences in climate, language, diet, and local culture over 
time.
17656
Which month is considered ideal for drying and preserving Panji 
manuscripts to protect them from moisture?
Bhadrapada (roughly 16 August to 15 September) when the sun is 
in Leo.
17657
What is the name of the 35th Mul that was added later as a high-
ranking branch?
Kharaur (a branch of the Phandah Mul).
17658
Term: 'Laukik'
Definition: The specific sub-categories or social standings within 
the Shrotriya Panji.
17659
Which Gotra is the only one found among the 'Karna Kayasthas' of 
Mithila?
Kashyapa
17660
The text notes that the Panji system was also once prevalent among 
which other community besides Brahmins and Kayasthas?
Kshatriyas (specifically Rajputs like the Gandhavariya).
17661
What was the title given to Modanand Jha for his expertise in the 
Panji system?
Panji-Shastra Martanda
17662
In the Shadvith list, which relative is the 'First Shadvith'?
The paternal great-great-grandfather (Vriddha Prapitamaha).
17663
In the Shadvith list, who is considered the 'Ninth Shadvith'?
The maternal grandfather's paternal great-grandfather.
17664
What is the 'Paanti' in the context of the Maithil social structure?
The social rank or row assigned to a family based on their 
genealogical record.
17665
Which historical ruler is credited with the formalisation of the Panji 
system in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva of the Karnata dynasty formalised the 
system.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17666
In the context of Panji, what does the term 'Uteá¸ h' represent?
It refers to the detailed introduction of 32 ancestral lineages 
(vamsas) required for marriage eligibility.
17667
According to the Panji rules, how many generations must be 
avoided for marriage on the maternal side?
Marriage is prohibited within five generations on the maternal side.
17668
According to the original Shastric rules, how many generations 
must be avoided for marriage on the paternal side?
Marriage is prohibited within seven generations on the paternal side.
17669
Which category of Maithil Brahmins is considered the highest in 
the Panji hierarchy?
The Shrotriya category is considered the highest.
17670
What are the three main social tiers of Maithil Brahmins defined by 
the Panji system?
The three tiers are Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
17671
How many 'Gotras' are recognised among Maithil Brahmins in the 
Panji system?
There are 20 recognised Gotras.
17672
What is the total number of 'Mools' (original village lineages) 
identified in the Maithil Brahmin community?
There are 167 identified Mools.
17673
In Maithil genealogy, who is a 'Biji Purush'?
The Biji Purush is the earliest known common ancestor of a 
specific lineage or Mool.
17674
The _____ is the name of the original ancestral village associated 
with a Maithil Brahmin's Mool.
Mool Gram
17675
What is the distinction between 'Tri-Pravara' and 'Panch-Pravara' in 
the Panji system?
It identifies whether a lineage has three or five distinct sage-
ancestors who composed Vedic hymns.
17676
Followers of the Samaveda Kauthuma shakha in Mithila are known 
by which specific term?
They are known as 'Chandogya'.
17677
Followers of the Shukla Yajurveda Madhyandina shakha in Mithila 
are known by which term?
They are known as 'Vajasaneyi'.
17678
What is a 'Siddhant Patra' (or Asvajan Patra) in the Panji system?
It is a certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride and 
groom are not prohibited relations.
17679
Who was appointed as the first 'Panjikar' for the Maithil Brahmin 
community?
Pandit Gunakar Jha was the first Panjikar.
17680
Which Gotra is universally held by all members of the Karna 
Kayastha community in Mithila?
All members belong to the Kashyapa Gotra.
17681
The 'Panch Gauda' Brahmins are geographically located to the north 
of which mountain range?
They are located north of the Vindhya Mountains.
17682
The 'Panch Dravida' Brahmins are geographically located to the 
south of which mountain range?
They are located south of the Vindhya Mountains.
17683
Who introduced the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) during the 18th 
century?
Maharaja Madhav Singh introduced the Shakha Panji.
17684
In which script are the original Panji records traditionally written?
They are written in the Tirhuta (Mithilakshar) script.
17685
What does the 'Dushan Panji' record in the Maithil genealogical 
system?
It records flaws or irregular marriages that occurred within a family 
lineage.
17686
What are the four essential ceremonies (Samskaras) for a 
'Chandogya' Brahmin student?
The four are Namakaran, Chudakaran, Upanayana, and 
Samavartana.
17687
The rule 'Matritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami' refers to what 
specific social restriction?
It refers to the prohibition of marriage within five maternal and 
seven paternal generations.
17688
Which philosopher mentioned 'Samuh Lekhya' (collective records) 
as early as the 7th century to protect lineage purity?
Kumarila Bhatta mentioned it in his work 'Tantra Vartika'.
17689
In the Panji system, what is the significance of the 'Chhaith'?
They are the 16 specific ancestral points (sixteen maternal and 
paternal lines) checked for marriage eligibility.
17690
What is the literal meaning of 'Panji' according to the source 
material?
It is a genealogical record where new births and marriages are 
continuously added.
17691
Which period is generally cited for the earliest recorded ancestors in 
the current Panji manuscripts?
The records trace back to approximately 450 AD.
17692
What happens to a Shrotriya family's rank if they marry into a 
lower-ranked Panjibaddh family?
The family's social rank declines through a process known as 
'Laukik' degradation.
17693
According to the mnemonic 'Jalat Rakshet, Tailat Rakshet', what 
must Panji books be protected from?
They must be protected from water, oil, and loose binding.
17694
During which solar month is it traditionally recommended to dry the 
Panji palm-leaf manuscripts?
It is recommended when the Sun enters the constellation of Leo 
(Sinha rashi).
17695
How does the Panji system define 'Svajana'?
A person who is a prohibited blood relative according to the 
Shastric generational rules.
17696
The 'Atyant Shrestha' category refers to the first tier of which 
genealogical unit?
It refers to the first tier of the 35 main Mools.
17697
How does the source material justify the comparison between Panji 
and modern 'Genome Mapping'?
Both systems track hereditary traits and genetic lineages to ensure 
healthy and lawful progeny.
17698
What are the two major divisions of Maithil Brahmins based on the 
Vedas they follow?
They are divided into Samavedic (Chandogya) and Yajurvedic 
(Vajasaneyi) branches.
17699
Why were 34 (later 35) Mools designated as 'Main Mools' out of the 
total 167?
They were selected based on their historical prominence and purity 
at the time of formalisation.
17700
Who was the designated Panjikar for the Kshatriya (Rajput) 
lineages in Mithila?
Vijayadatta was the first Panjikar for the Kshatriyas.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17701
Which specific ancestor is the 'first Chhaith' in the calculation for a 
bride's eligibility?
The paternal great-great-grandfather (Vriddha Prapitamaha).
17702
What is the 'Laukik' system within the Shrotriya Panji?
It is the record of status changes caused by marriage alliances 
between different family grades.
17703
The Panji system classifies Brahmin sub-groups as Uttakal, 
Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and _____.
Saraswat
17704
Which community's Panji records are currently considered largely 
extinct or obsolete according to the text?
The Panji records of the Kshatriya community have largely 
disappeared.
17705
What is the primary role of the 'Panjikar' in modern Maithil society? They act as genealogical experts who verify bloodlines and 
authorise marriage eligibility.
17706
How many 'Panch-Pravara' Gotras are mentioned in the Maithil 
Brahmin list?
There are three: Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaja (in certain 
variations).
17707
In the 18th century, which social factor led to the creation of 
'Shakha Panjis' for Shrotriyas?
The emergence of distinct hierarchy-based social classes within the 
community.
17708
What is the relationship between 'Mool' and 'Gotra' in the Panji 
system?
A single Gotra can contain multiple Mools, representing different 
ancestral villages.
17709
The 'Madhyam Mool' category includes how many specific lineages? It includes eight specific lineages.
17710
Which famous Maithili poet's lineage is extensively tracked across 
seven generations in the Panji records?
The poet Vidyapati Thakur's lineage is tracked.
17711
What was the year $1248$ Shake equivalent to in the Common Era 
calendar?
It is equivalent to $1326$ AD.
17712
What is the status of a child born from a 'Svajana' (prohibited) 
marriage according to ancient Smritis?
The child was traditionally considered 'Chandala' and excluded 
from Aryan social rites.
17713
Which sage is associated with the 'Sandilya' Gotra in the Panji list?
The sages are Sandilya, Asita, and Devala.
17714
Which Gotra is associated with the sages Shakti, Vashistha, and 
Parashara?
The Parashara Gotra.
17715
In the Panji system, the term 'Jaywar' refers to which group?
It refers to Brahmins who were not formally registered or 
'Panjibaddh' during the initial management.
17716
What is the primary difference in marriage timing between the 
Vajasaneyi and Chandogya branches?
Chandogya marriages must see the star Arundhati after sunset, 
while Vajasaneyi marriages have no such restriction.
17717
Which historical region is the geographic origin of the Maithil 
Brahmins?
The ancient Videha kingdom, now known as Mithila.
17718
How many total 'Panjis' are maintained for the Yogya and 
Panjibaddh categories?
There are 153 Panjis maintained for these categories.
17719
What does the term 'Pachhwari Paat' signify in the Panji context?
It refers to the lower or common grades of Brahmin lineages outside 
the Shrotriya elite.
17720
What does 'Pubari Paat' signify in the Panji context?
It refers to the high-ranking Shrotriya lineages.
17721
The 'Mool' of a person is determined by which ancestral factor?
It is determined by the original village name stated by their earliest 
known ancestor during the registration.
17722
Term: Siddhant
Definition: The final verification and approval given by a Panjikar 
for a marriage to proceed.
17723
In what year was the formal 'Panji Prabandh' (Genealogical 
Management) established in Mithila?
1326 AD (Shaka 1248).
17724
Which ruler is credited with initiating the Mithila Panji system to 
ensure genealogical purity?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
17725
What is the primary purpose of the 'Panji' system in Mithila society? To maintain 'jati shuddha' (caste purity) and prevent 
consanguineous marriages.
17726
The concept of _____ refers to the formal introduction of 32 
ancestors required for marriage eligibility.
Udegh
17727
According to Smriti rules used in Panji, how many generations 
must be avoided on the father's side for marriage?
Seven generations.
17728
According to Smriti rules used in Panji, how many generations 
must be avoided on the mother's side for marriage?
Five generations.
17729
In the Maithil Brahmin hierarchy, which group is considered the 
highest following the Panji reform?
Shrotriya
17730
What term is used for those Brahmins who are not registered in the 
formal Panji system?
Jaywar
17731
How many total Gotras (clans) are recognised within the Maithil 
Brahmin community?
20
17732
Which seven Gotras are considered the most prominent and 'well-
organised' in Mithila?
Sandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, Savarna, Parashara, Bharadwaja, and 
Katyayana.
17733
Term: Viji Purusha
Definition: The earliest known original ancestor of a specific 
lineage or clan.
17734
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Mool' signify?
The original ancestral village or seat of a family lineage.
17735
What is a 'Siddhant Patra' (also called Asajata Patra)?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a couple is 
not related within prohibited degrees.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17736
The professional genealogists responsible for maintaining the 
records and certifying marriages are called _____.
Panjikars
17737
Why is marriage prohibited between the Vatsa and Savarna Gotras 
in Mithila?
They share common ancestors (Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, 
Jamadagnya, and Apnuvana).
17738
The rule of _____ involves checking 16 specific ancestral positions 
to ensure no blood relation exists between a bride and groom.
Chhadh
17739
Which philosopher's 'Tantra Vartika' is cited as the earliest mention 
of 'Samuha Lekhya' (group lineage records)?
Kumarila Bhatta.
17740
Who was the first scholar appointed by King Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Panji of Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
17741
Who was the official appointed to manage the Panji for the 
Kshatriya community?
Vijaydatt.
17742
Which Brahmin sub-group is categorised as 'Pachhwari Paith'?
The Non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddh).
17743
Into how many hierarchical categories are the Shrotriya Brahmin 
Panjis divided?
Eight (known as Chauvala).
17744
How many 'Main Mools' were initially identified out of the 167 
total original villages?
34 (later increased to 35).
17745
How many Gotras are traditionally found within the Karna 
Kayastha community of Mithila?
One (Kashyapa Gotra).
17746
The 'Ayanti Shrestha' category represents the _____ class of 
original Brahmin lineages.
First
17747
What event reportedly triggered Maharaja Harisimhadeva to 
formalise the Panji system?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya unknowingly married his first cousin 
(maternal uncle's daughter).
17748
What is the distinction between 'Tri-pravara' and 'Pancha-pravara' 
lineages?
It refers to whether three or five distinct Vedic rishi ancestors are 
invoked in a Gotra's lineage.
17749
Identify the three Gotras that follow the 'Pancha-pravara' tradition.
Vatsa, Savarna, and Garga.
17750
To preserve the palm-leaf manuscripts, Panji books are traditionally 
dried when the Sun enters which zodiac sign?
Leo (Simha Rashi), usually between 16 August and 15 September.
17751
What does the term 'Pachhwari Paith' imply regarding the location 
of the Panji records?
Records of the Western (Pachhwari) group of Brahmins.
17752
The process of calculating 32 ancestral lineages to determine 
marriage eligibility is known as _____.
Uteht
17753
List the three ancestors associated with the Sandilya Gotra.
Sandilya, Asita, and Devala.
17754
The 'Dvitiya Shrestha' category of Mools includes lineages such as 
Rankoliwar and Paliwar, representing the _____ class.
Second
17755
What are 'Madhyam Mools' in the context of the Panji system?
Intermediate ancestral seats, such as Digkhwe and Belonche.
17756
In the Mithila hierarchy, what is the term for the 'marriage-eligible' 
category below Shrotriya?
Yogya
17757
Which script was traditionally used to record the Panji genealogies? Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
17758
The Panji records for the 'Pachhwari' group consist of _____ 
distinct books or registers.
153
17759
The Shrotriya group's records are limited to _____ distinct registers 
(Panjis).
32
17760
What is the 'Sakha Panji'?
A detailed branch-wise genealogy documenting titles, education, 
and maternal lineages.
17761
Who introduced the 'Sakha Panji' system during the reign of 
Maharaja Madhav Singh (c. 1760 AD)?
Panjikars acting under the King's order, which led to further sub-
divisions.
17762
The first 'Chhadh' position (Pratham Chhadh) to be checked is the 
_____.
Father's father's father (Prapitamaha).
17763
In the calculation of eligibility, what is the 'Sajata' status?
A condition where the bride and groom are found to be related 
within prohibited degrees, making marriage illegal.
17764
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Oini' and 'Mandas' Mools?
Kashyapa.
17765
The Gotra associated with the 'Ekahra' and 'Panchobh' Mools is 
_____.
Savarna
17766
Which Gotra includes the Mools of 'Sarisab', 'Mahua', and 'Pali'?
Sandilya.
17767
The Panji system classifies the Samavedi Brahmins of Mithila as 
_____.
Chhandogya
17768
The Panji system classifies the Shukla Yajurvedi Brahmins of 
Mithila as _____.
Vajasaneyi
17769
What major difference exists between Chhandogya and Vajasaneyi 
marriage rituals in Mithila?
Chhandogya marriages must be conducted after sunset after seeing 
the star Arundhati.
17770
How does the Panji define a 'Panjibaddh' Brahmin?
One whose name and complete genealogy are formally entered into 
the Panji records.
17771
Which major academic text on Dharma Shastra is mentioned as 
being written by Pt. Harinath Upadhyaya?
Smritisar.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17772
In the 16 'Chhadh' rules, if an ancestral position matches the 
groom's paternal side, the marriage is _____.
Prohibited (No eligibility).
17773
If a matching ancestral position ('Chhadh') is found on the groom's 
maternal side, is the marriage permitted?
Yes, provided it is beyond the 5th generation.
17774
What historical term is used to describe the entire collection of 
Maithil Brahmin lineages?
Pancha-Gaud Maithil.
17775
The original home of the Maithil Brahmins according to the text is 
the region between the Himalayas and the _____ river.
Ganga
17776
Which Gotra includes the rishis Bharadwaja, Angirasa, and 
Barhaspatya?
Bharadwaja.
17777
Which sage is the Viji Purusha of the 'Mandas' Mool in the 
Kashyapa Gotra?
Trilocana Bhatta.
17778
Who is the Viji Purusha of the 'Simhashram' Mool?
Mahamahopadhyaya Halayudha.
17779
In the Panji, the term 'Laukika' refers specifically to which 
category's records?
Shrotriya Brahmins.
17780
How many major 'Veda' branches are followed by Maithil 
Brahmins?
Two (Yajurveda and Samaveda).
17781
The Yajurveda followers in Mithila belong specifically to the _____ 
branch.
Madhyandina
17782
The Samaveda followers in Mithila belong specifically to the _____ 
branch.
Kauthuma
17783
Which ancestor is the common link for the 'Sarisab' lineage within 
the Sandilya Gotra?
Ratnapani.
17784
How many generations on the father's side did Maharaja Mahesh 
Thakur later prescribe for marriage eligibility?
The 7th generation is acceptable (avoiding up to the 6th).
17785
What is the 'Moolgram'?
The specific village where a 'Viji Purusha' (original ancestor) 
resided at the time of Panji's creation.
17786
The professional fee or gift given to a Panjikar for their services is 
considered _____.
Prasad (honorarium).
17787
Which Panji register records the 'upadhi' (titles) and educational 
achievements of ancestors?
Sakha Panji.
17788
What happens if a Brahmin marries a 'Sajata' (forbidden relative) 
according to ancient Mithila law?
The offspring were historically considered 'Chandal' and the father 
was ostracised from Aryan status.
17789
The Gotra of the great poet Vidyapati was _____.
Kashyapa (specifically the Bisapi branch).
17790
Identify the Gotra associated with the Mool 'Kujoli'.
Katyayana.
17791
Lineages from the 'Alambukaaksh' Gotra are associated with which 
Mools?
Basam, Katai, and Brahmapura.
17792
The 'Vatsa' Gotra includes which common Mools?
Pali, Hariambe, Tisauti, and Tarkar.
17793
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Brahmapura' Mool across 
multiple lineages?
Sandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, and Gautama.
17794
In the context of Panji, what is 'Uti' (or Up-Teep)?
A supplementary register or sub-branch record, often for Shrotriya 
lineages.
17795
Who was the Karnata dynasty King responsible for initiating the 
'Panji Prabandh' system in Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
17796
In what year ($AD$) was the formal Panji system established in 
Mithila?
$1326$ $AD$ (Shaka $1248$).
17797
What was the primary social objective behind the creation of the 
Panji Prabandh?
To maintain caste purity and prevent prohibited degrees of marriage.
17798
The term 'Udheá¹›h' in the Panji system refers to the introduction of 
how many distinct ancestral lines?
$32$ ancestral lines.
17799
How many total 'Gotras' are identified within the Maithil Brahmin 
community according to the text?
$20$.
17800
Which two Gotras are traditionally prohibited from intermarrying 
because they share the same ancestors despite having different 
names?
Vatsa and Savarna.
17801
According to the Panji rules, how many generations must be 
avoided for marriage on the paternal side?
$7$ generations (modified to $6$ in some practices).
17802
Who was the first 'Panjikar' (genealogist) appointed by the King for 
the Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
17803
Who was the first 'Panjikar' appointed for the Karna Kayastha 
community?
Shankardatta.
17804
Who was the first 'Panjikar' appointed for the Rajput (Kshatriya) 
community in Mithila?
Vijayadatta.
17805
What is the collective term for the five Northern Brahmin groups, 
which includes Maithils, Kanyakubjas, and Gaudas?
Pancha Gauda.
17806
What geographical feature serves as the boundary between the 
Pancha Gauda and Pancha Dravida Brahmin groups?
The Vindhya Mountains.
17807
Maithil Brahmins primarily follow which two Vedas?
Yajurveda and Samaveda.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17808
Followers of the Madhyandina branch of the White Yajurveda in 
Mithila are known as _____.
Vajasaneyi.
17809
Followers of the Kauthuma branch of the Samaveda in Mithila are 
known as _____.
Chhandoga.
17810
What are the four specific sacraments (Samskaras) for a Chhandoga 
Brahmin during Upanayana?
Namakarana, Chudakarana, Upanayana, and Samavartana.
17811
In the context of the Panji system, what is a 'Mula'?
The ancestral village or root identity of a family.
17812
How many 'Mulas' (ancestral roots) were originally identified in the 
Maithil Brahmin Panji?
$167$.
17813
What is the difference between 'Mula' and 'Mula Grama'?
Mula is the original ancestral village; Mula Grama is the village 
where the family currently resides.
17814
A 'Tri-pravara' Gotra is one whose ancestors composed _____ 
distinct Suktas of the Rigveda.
$3$.
17815
A 'Pancha-pravara' Gotra is one whose ancestors composed _____ 
distinct Suktas of the Rigveda.
$5$.
17816
What are the three highest social categories of Maithil Brahmins as 
defined by the Panji system?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
17817
What term is used for those Maithil Brahmins whose names are not 
recorded in the genealogical registers?
Jaybar.
17818
Which specific Panji records the faults, character issues, or 
prohibited marriages that occurred within a lineage?
Dushana Panji.
17819
Under which Maharaja's reign was the 'Shakha Panji' (branch 
register) introduced to further classify the community?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
17820
What is a 'Siddhanta Patra' (or Asawajanya Patra)?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a proposed 
marriage is not within prohibited degrees.
17821
The calculation of 'Chadhvitha' involves checking _____ specific 
ancestral relations for marriage eligibility.
$16$.
17822
In 'Chadhvitha', the 1st check refers to the father of the bride's 
_____.
Great-great-grandfather.
17823
What is the function of the 'Mula Panji'?
It records the original stock and expansion of various family roots.
17824
What is the function of the 'Gotra Panji'?
It lists all the Gotras and their corresponding ancient Mulas.
17825
Which Vedic branch includes 'Vedarambha' as one of the four 
Upanayana sacraments?
Vajasaneyi.
17826
How are Shrotriya Brahmins geographically distinguished from 
others in Mithila?
They are sometimes called 'Pubaripaat', while others are 
'Pachhuaripaat'.
17827
How many sub-categories (Chauvala) are the Shrotriya Panjis 
divided into?
$8$ categories.
17828
How many sub-categories exist for the 'Yogya' and 'Vanshaja' 
(Panjibaddha) Brahmins?
$15$ categories.
17829
Who was the 14th-century scholar whose unintended marriage to a 
close relative spurred the formalisation of the Panji records?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya.
17830
What is the 'Biji Purusha' in a Maithil lineage?
The earliest known common ancestor of a specific Mula.
17831
The Panji system checks for 'Sapinda' relationships. What does 
'Sapinda' mean?
Individuals who share a common ancestor and are thus forbidden to 
marry.
17832
Which script was traditionally used to maintain the Panji records in 
Mithila?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
17833
What is the significance of the solar month of Simha (August-
September) for Panji books?
It is the time when the palm-leaf or paper books are dried in the sun 
to preserve them.
17834
What is a 'Laukika' in the context of Shrotriya genealogy?
A specific branch or family identity within the Shrotriya category.
17835
Which Gotra includes the Mulas of 'Dighva' (Dighwe) and 'Sarisab'? Shandilya.
17836
Which Gotra includes the Mulas of 'Oini' and 'Mandu'?
Kashyapa.
17837
Which Gotra includes the Mulas of 'Pali' and 'Hariamb'?
Vatsa.
17838
Which Gotra includes the Mula 'Ekahara'?
Bharadwaj (also Kaundinya).
17839
What is the specific rule regarding marriage and the maternal 
uncle's children in Mithila?
Marriage with the offspring of the maternal uncle is strictly 
prohibited.
17840
In the Panji system, what happens to a family's status if they marry 
into a lower category?
The family status descends (Adhogami), and they may eventually 
lose their Shrotriya rank.
17841
How many main 'Mulas' were selected as 'Atyant Shrestha' (first 
grade) lineages?
$13$.
17842
What is the 'Shakha Panji'?
A register that tracks the descendants and marriages of specific 
branches of a family tree.
17843
Which historical region's capital was Mithila, where these Brahmin 
traditions flourished?
Videha.
17844
What does the Panji indicate about the status of Maithil Kayasthas 
(Karna Kayasthas)?
They have only one Gotra (Kashyapa) and use Mula to determine 
marriage eligibility.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17845
According to the 'Tantra Vartika', why did King Harisimhadeva 
formalise the 'Samuha-lekhya'?
To ensure that people did not forget their paternal and maternal 
lineages.
17846
Who was assigned to supervise the Panji of the Kshatriyas 
(Rajputs) in Mithila?
Vijayadatta.
17847
A marriage in Mithila is considered 'Ashastriya' (against scripture) 
if _____ is not obtained.
Siddhanta (official clearance from a Panjikar).
17848
What does 'Swarajana' mean in the context of marriage laws?
A person belonging to the same prohibited ancestral group.
17849
The record of maternal and paternal ancestors for marriage is called 
_____.
Panji.
17850
In the 16 'Chadhvitha' checks, what is the 9th check?
Paternal grandfather of the bride's maternal grandfather.
17851
How does the Panji system describe the marriage of 'Chaitanya 
Mahaprabhu's' guru, Ramapati Upadhyaya?
He was a Maithil Brahmin of the Vatsa Gotra (Kariama Mula) who 
settled in Bengal.
17852
What is the significance of the year $1150$ Shaka ($1228$ $AD$) 
in the text?
It is mentioned in relation to the genealogies of specific lineages 
like Mandu Mula.
17853
Why is 'Brahmapura' Mula unique in the Maithil Panji?
It is found across multiple Gotras, including Shandilya, Vatsa, and 
Kashyapa.
17854
What is the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandha' established in 
Mithila?
To maintain genealogical records and ensure caste purity for 
marriage eligibility.
17855
During which century was the Panji system formally established 
under the patronage of King Harisimhadeva?
The 14th Century (specifically 1326 AD / Shaka 1248).
17856
Which historical event triggered the formalisation of the Panji 
records?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya unknowingly married a close relative 
due to a lack of genealogical records.
17857
Who was the Karnat dynasty king credited with initiating the Panji 
Prabandha?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
17858
What term refers to the professional genealogists appointed by the 
king to maintain these records?
Panjikara.
17859
Maithil Brahmins are divided into two main Vedic branches; name 
the branch that follows the 'Yajurveda'.
Vajasaneyi (Madhyandina Shakha).
17860
Maithil Brahmins following the 'Samaveda' (Kauthuma Shakha) are 
commonly known as _____.
Chandoga.
17861
In the Panji system, what is the 'Udeha'?
A detailed record of 32 ancestral lineages used to verify marriage 
eligibility.
17862
What is the 'Siddhanta Patra' (or Aswajanapatra)?
