A formal certificate issued by a Panjikara confirming that a bride 
and groom are not closely related.
17863
Define the term 'Swajana' in the context of Mithila marriage laws.
A person with whom marriage is prohibited due to prohibited blood 
relation (Sapinda).
17864
How many total 'Gotras' are recognised among Maithil Brahmins 
according to the source?
20
17865
Which seven 'Gotras' are considered the most prominent and 
systematic in the Maithil Brahmin community?
Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, Savarna, Parashara, Bharadwaja, and 
Katyayana.
17866
In Maithil genealogy, what does 'Moola' signify?
The original ancestral village or root of a lineage.
17867
How many 'Moolas' (original roots) were initially identified and 
recorded?
167
17868
Out of the 167 'Moolas', how many are considered the 'Main 
Moolas' (Mukhya Moola)?
34 (later 35 including the Phandah branch).
17869
The classification of Maithil Brahmins into Shrotriya, Yogya, and 
Panjibaddha occurred during the reign of which king?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
17870
Which group occupies the highest tier in the social hierarchy of 
Maithil Brahmins established by Maharaja Madhav Singh?
Shrotriya.
17871
What is the gap requirement for marriage on the paternal side for 
'Shrotriya' Brahmins?
Seven generations.
17872
What is the gap requirement for marriage on the maternal side for 
'Shrotriya' Brahmins?
Five generations.
17873
How many generations must be excluded on the paternal side for 
'Panjibaddha' (and others) for marriage eligibility?
Six generations.
17874
Define 'Pravara' in the context of Brahmin lineages.
The group of illustrious rishi ancestors associated with a specific 
Gotra.
17875
Which three Gotras are classified as 'Pancha-pravara'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Garga.
17876
According to the Panji rules, marriage is prohibited if the bride and 
groom share the same _____ or _____.
Gotra; Pravara.
17877
What is the 'Chhattha' (Sixth) rule in Maithil marriage eligibility?
Prohibition of marriage if any of the 16 identified 'Chhattha' 
ancestors of the bride appear in the groom's paternal lineage.
17878
Under the Panji rules, can a person marry the child of their step-
mother's brother?
No, this is explicitly prohibited.
17879
What does 'Sakha Panji' record?
The expanded lineages of families as they moved from their original 
Moola to new villages.
17880
What is the 'Dooshana Panji' used for?
Recording 'faults' or deviations in a lineage to warn future 
generations.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17881
Which king of the Karnat dynasty is described as the 'Chudamani' 
(Crest-jewel)?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
17882
Who was the first scholar appointed to head the Panji management 
for Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
17883
Which group within Maithil society also maintained Panji records 
initially but later discontinued them?
Kshatriyas (Rajputs).
17884
What is the 'Laukika' in the context of Shrotriya Brahmins?
Specific sub-classifications or records within the Shrotriya Panji.
17885
How many 'Chhattha' (sixth-position) ancestors are verified for a 
bride during marriage eligibility checks?
16
17886
The rule 'Matritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami' refers to the 
avoidance of how many generations?
Five from the mother's side and seven from the father's side.
17887
Who modified the paternal exclusion rule to six generations, which 
became widely accepted in Mithila?
Mahamahopadhyaya Mahesh Thakur.
17888
What term is used for Brahmins whose names were successfully 
recorded in the Panji registers?
Panjibaddha.
17889
What term was used for those not included in the formal Panji 
system?
Jaibar (or Jayewar).
17890
The 'Pancha Gauda' Brahmins include the Maithil, Saraswat, 
Kanyakubja, Gauda, and _____ communities.
Utkala.
17891
What is the geographical boundary between 'Pancha Gauda' and 
'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhya Mountains.
17892
Which Gotra includes the Moolas of 'Dighe', 'Sarisab', and 'Mahua'? Shandilya.
17893
To which Gotra does the 'Oini' Moola belong?
Kashyapa.
17894
Which script was traditionally used to write the Panji books?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
17895
What preservation method is advised for Panji books in the 
mnemonic 'Jalat Rakshe Tailat Rakshe'?
Protect from water, oil, and loose binding.
17896
During which solar month should Panji books be dried in the sun 
for preservation?
When the sun is in Leo (Singh Rashi), typically mid-August to mid-
September.
17897
What is the 'Ayaanti Shrestha' tier in Moola classification?
The first/highest grade of Moolas (including Khadaure, Khauade, 
etc.).
17898
Name the second tier of Moolas mentioned in the text.
Dvadvitiya (including lineage-based moolas like Ranwalihar, etc.).
17899
What are 'Madhyam Moolas'?
Middle-tier Moolas such as Digher, Belaunch, and Panichobh.
17900
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Pali', 'Hariambe', and 'Takarwal' 
Moolas?
Vatsa.
17901
The Gotra 'Alambukaksha' contains which main Moola?
Basaam.
17902
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Nikhuti' Moola?
Kaushika.
17903
The 'Ekahara' and 'Pali' Moolas are found under which Gotra?
Kaundinya.
17904
What is unique about the marriage of Samavedi Brahmins 
(Chandoga) regarding timing?
The 'Uttara Vivaha' must take place after sunset after sighting the 
star Arundhati.
17905
Who is the famous Maithili poet whose 19-generation genealogy is 
preserved in the Panji?
Vidyapati Thakur.
17906
Which Moola is associated with the great scholar and reformer 
Mahesh Thakur?
Khadaure.
17907
What is 'Samuhalakhya', as mentioned by Kumarila Bhatta?
Ancient collective records of families used to preserve lineage 
history.
17908
Why is marriage prohibited between 'Savarna' and 'Vatsa' Gotras?
They are considered to share the same ancestral rishis (equivalent to 
being the same Gotra).
17909
In the verification process, how many paternal ancestors of the 
groom are checked against the bride's 'Chhattha' list?
Six generations.
17910
What is the 'Paanti' (or Panthi) in the Maithil Brahmin hierarchy?
The social rank or status assigned based on genealogical purity and 
conduct.
17911
Which group is referred to as 'Pubari Paanti'?
Shrotriya Brahmins.
17912
Which group is referred to as 'Pachhabari Paanti'?
Non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and others).
17913
What does the term 'Moola-Gram' represent in a Siddhanta record?
The combination of the original lineage root and the current village 
of residence.
17914
The text describes the Panji system as a precursor to what modern 
scientific concept?
Genome Mapping.
17915
Which kingâ€™s court poet, Jyotirishwar Thakur, mentions 
Harisimhadeva in the 'Varna Ratnakara'?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva himself (he is the protagonist/hero).
17916
How many Rajput (Kshatriya) clans are listed in the 'Varna 
Ratnakara' context?
72 clans (with 64 detailed).
17917
What was the role of 'Vijayadatta' in Harisimhadeva's court?
Managing the Panji records for the Kshatriya community.
17918
Why is marriage prohibited with a daughter of the mother's brother 
(Matul Kanya) in Panji law?
It violates the maternal generation gap (Sapinda) rules.
17919
What is the significance of the year 1248 Shaka in Mithila history?
It is the year the Panji Prabandha was officially instituted.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17920
What is 'Ayaanchi'?
A title or status referring to those who do not ask for anything 
(associated with Pandit Bhavanath Mishra).
17921
Define 'Chaurasi' (or Chauala) in the context of Shrotriya rankings.
The specific hierarchical categories or 'groups' within the Shrotriya 
community.
17922
How many total Shrotriya Panjis are recorded in the system?
32
17923
How many Panjis are recorded for the Yogya and Panjibaddha 
categories?
153
17924
The 'Vatsa' Gotra includes rishis like Chyavana, Jamadagni, and 
_____.
Aurva.
17925
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Kushika', 'Atri', and 'Jamadagni' 
Pravaras?
Kaushika.
17926
What is 'Tirhuta' primarily considered in the context of Panji 
preservation?
The 'soul' or lifeline of the Panji Prabandha system.
17927
Which title was given to Modanand Jha for his expertise in Panji 
Shastra?
Panji Shastra Martand.
17928
The Panji system prevents marriage with the children of the father's 
sister (Pitri-swasa). True or False?
True.
17929
What is 'Karna Kayastha'?
The specific Kayastha community in Mithila that shares the Panji 
system with Brahmins.
17930
Which single Gotra is predominant among Karna Kayasthas?
Kashyapa.
17931
Which three social groups in Mithila were primarily covered by the 
Panji Prabandh?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
17932
What does the term 'Udegh' refer to in the context of Mithila 
genealogy?
The introduction of seven generations of ancestors required for 
marriage eligibility.
17933
In the Panji system, how many maternal and paternal ancestral lines 
must be verified for a marriage to be valid?
A total of thirty-two ancestral lines (Introduction of 32 ancestors).
17934
Into which three hierarchical categories were Maithil Brahmins 
divided during the Panji classification?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
17935
What term is used for Brahmins who are not officially recorded in 
the Panji system?
Jaywar.
17936
According to traditional rules, how many generations must be 
avoided on the paternal side for a marriage to be permissible?
Seven generations (later modified to six in practice).
17937
How many generations must be avoided on the maternal side 
(Matripaksha) for a marriage in the Maithil Brahmin community?
Five generations.
17938
How many total 'Gotras' (clans) are recognized among Maithil 
Brahmins?
Twenty.
17939
The Maithil Brahmin community recognizes $167$ fundamental 
units known as _____.
Mools (or Moolgrams).
17940
How many 'main' Mools were originally identified from the total of 
$167$?
Thirty-four (later thirty-five with the addition of the 'Khama' 
branch).
17941
Which king of the Karnat dynasty formalized the Panji Prabandh 
system?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
17942
In which year (AD) was the Panji Prabandh system officially 
established in Mithila?
1326 AD (Saka 1248).
17943
Who was the scholar appointed by King Harisimhadeva to manage 
the Brahmin Panji Prabandh?
Pandit Gunakar Jha.
17944
Who was appointed as the official genealogist for the Kshatriya 
community in Mithila?
Vijayadatta.
17945
What are the two Vedic branches followed by Maithil Brahmins?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina branch) and Samaveda (Kauthuma 
branch).
17946
Maithil Brahmins who follow the Samaveda are referred to as 
_____.
Chandog.
17947
Maithil Brahmins who follow the Yajurveda are referred to as 
_____.
Vajasaneyi.
17948
What term refers to the earliest known ancestor of a specific lineage 
in a Panji record?
Beeji Purush.
17949
What is the 'Moolgram' in the Panji system?
The original village or ancestral home of a family lineage.
17950
The five northern Brahmin groups, including Maithils, are 
collectively known as the _____.
Pancha-Gauda.
17951
Which geographical feature serves as the dividing line between the 
Pancha-Gauda and Pancha-Dravida Brahmin groups?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
17952
What is the primary difference between a 'Tri-pravar' and a 'Panch-
pravar' lineage?
Tri-pravar lineages name three sage ancestors, while Panch-pravar 
name five.
17953
List the three major gotras that fall under the 'Panch-pravar' 
category.
Vatsa, Savarna, and Gargya.
17954
What script is traditionally used to write the Panji records?
Tirhuta (also known as Mithilakshar).
17955
What is a 'Siddhanta Patra' (or Ashvajana-patra)?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride 
and groom are not related by blood.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17956
Why is marriage prohibited between the Savarna and Vatsa gotras?
Because they share the same common ancestors (Pravaras).
17957
What was the inspiration behind the formalisation of Panji records 
in the 14th century?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya unknowingly married his first cousin 
due to lack of records.
17958
What is the 'Chhadwath' in the Panji evaluation process?
The sixteen critical ancestral points (sixth-degree ancestors) 
checked for commonality.
17959
What is the specific rule regarding marriage to a daughter of a 
maternal uncle (Mama)?
It is strictly prohibited.
17960
What is a 'Sakha Panji'?
A branch genealogy record initiated during the reign of Maharaja 
Madhav Singh in 1760 AD.
17961
What is a 'Dushan Panji'?
A record detailing 'flaws' or social/moral transgressions within a 
family lineage.
17962
Maithil Brahmins of the Shrotriya class are also referred to as 
_____.
Pubaripar (Easterners).
17963
Non-Shrotriya Maithil Brahmins are sometimes referred to by the 
term _____.
Pachhabaripar (Westerners).
17964
How many hierarchical categories (Chaupala) exist within the 
Shrotriya Panji?
Eight.
17965
What is the 'Adhikar Mala'?
A list prepared by a Panjikar for a father, detailing eligible grooms 
for his daughter.
17966
Which Sanskrit verse provides the rule for preserving Panji books?
Jalat Rakshet Tailat Rakshet (Protect from water, protect from oil).
17967
According to the Panji system, when is the best time to dry 
genealogical manuscripts?
When the Sun is in the Leo (Simha) constellation (approx. August-
September).
17968
Which famous Maithil poet's lineage is meticulously preserved in 
the Panji records for several centuries?
Vidyapati Thakur.
17969
The title of the source material 'Genome Mapping' refers to which 
historical practice?
The recording of genealogical data in the Panji system over $1,500$ 
years.
17970
Which major Maithil Brahmin scholar wrote the 'Smritisar'?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya.
17971
How many Pravaras are associated with the 'Shandilya' gotra?
Three (Shandilya, Asit, and Deval).
17972
In the Maithil Brahmin community, which gotra is the only one 
recognized for Karna Kayasthas?
Kashyapa.
17973
The 'Laukik' refers to which specific type of Panji record?
The Shrotriya Panji.
17974
What is the 'Pari-Panji'?
A record used to track original villages and habitation history, 
prevalent for the last $500$ years.
17975
Who issued the order to compile the 'Sakha Panjis' which led to 
further social stratification?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
17976
The rule 'Matritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami' refers to what?
Avoiding five maternal generations and seven paternal generations 
in marriage.
17977
What was the historical capital of the Videha dynasty, which later 
became synonymous with Mithila?
Janakpur.
17978
Which branch of Yajurveda is specifically mentioned as being 
followed by Maithils?
Madhyandina Shakha.
17979
The Samavedi Maithil Brahmins follow the _____ branch of the 
Veda.
Kauthuma Shakha.
17980
What are the four main sacraments (Sanskars) performed for a 
'Chandog' Brahmin during Upanayana?
Namkaran, Chura-karan, Upanayana, and Samavartan.
17981
How does the Upanayana process for a 'Vajasaneyi' Brahmin differ 
from a 'Chandog'?
Vajasaneyi includes 'Vedarambh' as one of the four key sacraments.
17982
Which philosophical text by Kumarila Bhatta is cited as the earliest 
mention of 'Samuh-Lekhya' (lineage records)?
Tantra Vartika.
17983
In the context of Panji records, what is a 'Behat'?
A sub-branch or local derivation of a Moolà¦—à§ à¦°à¦¾à¦®.
17984
Why is the Maithil Panji system considered 'scientific' by the 
author?
Because it functions as a biological/genetic tracking system to 
prevent inbreeding.
17985
Which social class within Maithil Brahmins was historically 
identified by their high scholarly and ritual standards?
Shrotriya.
17986
The term 'Beeji' literally translates to _____.
Seed.
17987
What was the significance of Maharaja Kameshwar Singh in the 
history of Panjis?
He was the patron who awarded the title 'Panji-Shastra Martand' to 
expert genealogists.
17988
Which script-related movement does the author link to the 
preservation of Panji books?
The preservation of the Tirhuta (Mithilakshar) script.
17989
What is the primary reason the Panji system was not maintained 
among Kshatriyas over time?
Changes in political status and the loss of systematic scholarly 
patronage.
17990
If a bride is the 'Chhadwath' of the groom's paternal side, is the 
marriage allowed?
No, it is strictly forbidden.
17991
If a common ancestor appears in the maternal line (Matripaksha) 
for both parties at the sixth generation, is marriage permissible?
Yes, as the maternal prohibition only extends to the fifth generation.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
17992
Which Maithil Brahmin scholar was the teacher of Chaitanya 
Mahaprabhu?
Ishvara Puri (or Paramananda Puri, associated with the Vatsa gotra 
and Kimah mool).
17993
List four of the eight 'Medium Mools' mentioned in the text.
Dighare, Belaunchi, Ekahate, and Panchobhe.
17994
What does 'Sapiá¹‡á¸ a' signify in the context of Hindu marriage 
laws used in Panji?
People sharing the same body particles or having common ancestors.
17995
The first 'Chhadwath' represents the relationship to which ancestor?
The paternal great-great-great grandfather (Vriddha-Prapitamaha).
17996
According to the Panji theory, why did regional Brahmin subgroups 
emerge?
Due to climatic differences, food habits, language, and geographic 
isolation.
17997
Which gotra is described as having $74$ branches spread across 
Mithila?
Kashyapa (specifically the Mandar branch).
17998
What term is used for those who issue the 'Siddhanta Patra'?
Panjikar.
17999
The Maithil Panji system is estimated to track lineage spanning 
more than _____ years.
One thousand.
18000
What is the 'Laukik' status in a Shrotriya family?
The relative social standing or honor of a family based on their past 
marriage alliances.
18001
According to the text, what happened to a person who married a 
'Swajana' (forbidden relative)?
They were treated as outcastes and their children were considered 
'Chandala' in status.
18002
Which historical figure's lineage in the Panji is used to calculate the 
date of ancient Indian astronomers like Aryabhatta?
The Mandar Mool lineage (linking to various 'Bhattas').
18003
During which historical period was the Panji Prabandh system 
initially created?
The medieval period (specifically around the 14th century AD).
18004
Which ruler of the Karnat dynasty is credited with the formalisation 
of the Panji system in Mithila?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
18005
In which Shaka year was the Panji system officially instituted?
Shaka 1248 (corresponding to 1326 AD).
18006
What was the primary social objective of creating the Panji 
Prabandh?
To ensure 'Jati Shuddhi' (purity of caste) for Brahmins, Kayasthas, 
and Kshatriyas.
18007
What does the term 'Udrehr' refer to in the context of Mithila 
genealogy?
The introduction and identification of 32 ancestors required for 
marriage eligibility.
18008
According to the Panji rules, how many generations must be 
avoided on the paternal side for a valid marriage?
Seven generations.
18009
How many generations must be avoided on the maternal side for a 
valid marriage in the Panji system?
Five generations.
18010
Name the four hierarchical categories into which Maithil Brahmins 
were divided following the Panji implementation.
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddh, and Jaywar.
18011
What is the total number of Gotras identified among Maithil 
Brahmins in the source text?
20 Gotras.
18012
How many 'Mula Grams' (original ancestral villages) were 
identified during the Panji Prabandh?
167 Mula Grams.
18013
Which Vedic branch do 'Vajasaneyi' Brahmins follow?
The Madhyandini Shakha of the Shukla Yajurveda.
18014
Which Vedic branch do 'Chandoga' Brahmins follow?
The Kauthuma Shakha of the Samaveda.
18015
What distinguishes 'Tri-pravara' from 'Panch-pravara' in the Gotra 
system?
Tri-pravara identifies three specific ancestors as Rigvedic seers, 
while Panch-pravara identifies five.
18016
Why is marriage prohibited between individuals of the Vatsa and 
Savarna Gotras despite being different Gotras?
They share the same set of ancestors (Pravaras).
18017
Who was the scholar appointed by Maharaja Harisimhadeva to lead 
the Panji documentation for Brahmins?
Saduphadhyaya Gunakar Jha.
18018
Which scholar's work, the 'Tantra Varttika', is cited as an early 
historical reference for recorded lineage (Samuha Lekhya)?
Kumarila Bhatta.
18019
The calculation of _____ involves checking 16 specific ancestral 
relations to verify marriage compatibility.
Chadvith
18020
What is the common Gotra found among Karna Kayasthas 
according to the text?
Kashyapa.
18021
Which Panji specifically records 'Kshana' (defects or social lapses) 
within a family lineage?
Dushan Panji.
18022
What is the significance of the 'Beeji Purusha' in a family's 
genealogy?
He is the earliest known ancestor from whom the lineage is 
officially tracked.
18023
Which month of the year is traditionally recommended for drying 
Panji manuscripts to protect them from moisture?
When the Sun is in the Leo (Simha) rashi, roughly from 16 August 
to 15 September.
18024
The Pancha Gauda group of Brahmins includes Utkala, 
Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and _____.
Saraswat
18025
What is the primary difference between a 'Mula Panji' and a 
'Shakha Panji'?
Mula Panji focuses on ancestral origins, while Shakha Panji details 
the expanded branches of families over time.
18026
Concept: Samuha Lekhya
Definition: A collective written record of lineage used by Brahmins 
and Kshatriyas to maintain family history and purity.
18027
Which specific ancestor in the calculation of Chadvith is known as 
the 'First Chadvith'?
The paternal great-grandfather's grandfather (Vriddha 
Prapitamaha's Pitamaha).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18028
Under which condition does a relation discovered in the Chadvith 
calculation allow a marriage to proceed?
If the relation is found on the maternal side (Matripaksha) rather 
than the paternal side (Pitripaksha).
18029
Name the script traditionally used to write and maintain the Panji 
manuscripts.
Tirhuta (Mithilakshara).
18030
According to Maharaja Harisimhadeva's decree, a marriage 
performed without a _____ is considered unscriptural.
Siddhanta (official genealogical verification)
18031
Which type of Panji documents the female ancestors of a lineage?
Strikartak (found within Udrehr Panjis).
18032
How many main 'Mulas' (villages of origin) were designated as the 
primary 34/35 units of the system?
35 (including the Khanam branch of Phandah Mula).
18033
The Gotras of Vatsa, Savarna, and _____ are the only Maithil 
Brahmin Gotras following the Panch-pravara system.
Bhargava
18034
What title is given to the professional genealogists who maintain 
and verify the Panji records?
Panjikar.
18035
Who was the main examiner for the Panji Shastra Martand exams 
under Maharaja Kameshwar Singh?
Mahamahopadhyaya Dr Sir Ganganath Jha.
18036
What historical event in the 14th century prompted the formal 
institutionalisation of the Panji system?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya inadvertently marrying his cousin due 
to lack of recorded lineage.
18037
The term 'Jaywar' refers to Brahmins who were not _____ during 
the initial registration.
Panjibaddh (registered in the Panji books)
18038
Which scholar in the 18th century was commissioned by Maharaja 
Madhav Singh to create the 'Shakha Panji'?
Panji Pachanand Devanand Jha (also known as Chhoti Jha).
18039
In the Chadvith system, how many maternal-side ancestors are 
traditionally included in the calculation?
Five generations.
18040
The 16th Chadvith calculation involves the _____.
Maternal grandmother's maternal grandmother's maternal 
grandfather.
18041
What is the role of the 'Gotra Panji'?
To list all Gotras and their corresponding ancient Mula origins.
18042
Name one of the three threats to Panji manuscripts mentioned in the 
traditional mnemonic 'Jalat Raksha, Tailat Raksha'.
Water (Jala), Oil (Taila), or Loose binding (Sthula Bandhanat).
18043
Which specific branch of the Karnat dynasty ruled Mithila when the 
Panji system began?
Karnat Vanshiya Kshatriya Maharaja.
18044
The Panji system acts as a record of marriage prohibitions based on 
_____ (shared ancestry).
Sapinda
18045
Who was appointed to manage the Panji records for the Kayastha 
community?
Shankardatta.
18046
According to the text, which caste group has largely lost its Panji 
records in the modern era?
Kshatriyas.
18047
What is the 'Laukika' in the context of Shrotriya Panji?
The detailed genealogy of the Shrotriya class, divided into eight 
Chaurals.
18048
How many Shrotriya Panjis were historically recorded?
32 Panjis.
18049
How many Yogya and Panjibaddh Panjis were historically 
recorded?
153 Panjis.
18050
The 'Asvajana Patra' is another name for which document?
The Siddhanta Patra.
18051
What is the historical significance of the 'Tantra Varttika' regarding 
the Panji?
It provides the earliest textual mention of 'Samuha Lekhya' (group 
records) in the 7th century.
18052
Which Gotra is linked to the ancestors Shandilya, Asita, and 
Devala?
Shandilya.
18053
Which Gotra is linked to the ancestors Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, 
Jamadagnya, and Apnuvana?
Vatsa (or Savarna).
18054
Which Gotra includes the Pravara ancestors Shakti, Vashishtha, and 
Parashara?
Parashara.
18055
Which Gotra includes the Pravara ancestors Katyayana, Vishnu, 
and Angirasa?
Katyayana.
18056
Why is the Shakha Panji of 1690-1710 AD by Madhusudan Jha 
considered unique?
It is written on palm leaves (Talpatra) and is one of the best-
preserved early records.
18057
How does the Panji define the 'Paternal Side' for marriage 
calculations?
The six generations of the father's lineage (Pitripaksha).
18058
How does the Panji define the 'Maternal Side' for marriage 
calculations?
The five generations of the mother's lineage (Matripaksha).
18059
Which scholar is credited with the 19th generation preservation of 
the Panji records starting from Gunakar Jha?
Vidyanand Jha.
18060
What is the consequence of marrying a 'Svajana' (forbidden 
relative) according to the Smritis cited?
The offspring are considered 'Chandala' and the parents are 
ostracised from the Arya dignity.
18061
The 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins are located south of which 
geographic landmark?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
18062
The 'Pancha Gauda' Brahmins are located north of which 
geographic landmark?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
18063
What does the Panji system identify as the 'root of Dharma'?
The purity of the Jati (caste).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18064
According to the Panji rules, a bride cannot be the daughter of the 
groom's _____.
Maternal uncle (Matamaha).
18065
Which term describes the process of calculating blood relations 
across 32 lineages?
Udrehr Panji registration.
18066
In what year of the Common Era was the Mithila Panji system 
formally established?
1326 AD
18067
Which ruler of the Karnat dynasty is credited with initiating the 
Panji Prabandh system?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva
18068
What was the primary objective of the Panji system according to 
the source material?
To maintain the purity of the caste and regulate prohibited degrees 
of relationship in marriage.
18069
The identification of 32 ancestral branches required for Brahmin 
marriage verification is known as _____.
Uderh
18070
In the Panji system, the earliest known progenitor of a lineage 
branch is called the _____.
Beeji Purush
18071
According to Dharmashastra rules in the Panji, how many 
generations must be avoided on the paternal side for a marriage to 
be valid?
Seven
18072
According to Dharmashastra rules in the Panji, how many 
generations must be avoided on the maternal side for a marriage to 
be valid?
Five
18073
How many 'Gotras' (clans) are traditionally identified among 
Maithil Brahmins in the Panji system?
20
18074
The term 'Mool' in the Mithila genealogical records refers to the 
family's _____.
Ancestral village or place of origin.
18075
What certificate must be obtained from a Panjikar to confirm that a 
bride and groom are not related within prohibited degrees?
Siddhanta Patra (or Aswajanapatra)
18076
Which social group, in addition to Brahmins and Kayasthas, was 
originally included in the Panji system during its inception?
Kshatriyas
18077
Who was appointed as the first Panjikar for Maithil Brahmins by 
King Harisimhadeva?
Gunakar Jha
18078
Who was the first appointed Panjikar for the Karn Kayastha 
community?
Shankardutta
18079
Term: Pravara
Definition: The specific ancestral sages associated with a Gotra 
who were composers of Vedic hymns.
18080
What are the two specific types of Pravara classifications found in 
Maithil Brahmins based on the number of sages?
Tri-pravara (three sages) and Panch-pravara (five sages).
18081
Which three Gotras among the Maithil Brahmins fall under the 
'Panch-pravara' category?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Garga.
18082
Maithil Brahmins are primarily followers of which two Vedas?
Yajurveda and Samaveda.
18083
Followers of the Madhyandini branch of the White Yajurveda in 
Mithila are known as _____.
Vajasaneyi
18084
How many 'Mool-grams' (original villages) were identified at the 
time of the Panji's creation?
167
18085
What is the function of the 'Dooshan Panji' within the genealogical 
records?
To record flaws, social exclusions, or 'doshas' within a lineage.
18086
Which type of Panji focuses on recording the current descendants 
and branches of a specific family lineage?
Shakha Panji
18087
In the calculation of 'Chhadwith', how many specific maternal and 
paternal relations are scrutinised?
16
18088
The script traditionally used to maintain and write the Panji records 
is called _____.
Tirhuta (or Mithilakshar)
18089
What historical event led to the institutionalisation of the Panji 
system by King Harisimhadeva?
A high-ranking scholar inadvertently married a close relative due to 
a lack of genealogical records.
18090
According to the Panji system, the bride must not be a descendant 
of the groom's _____.
Maternal uncle.
18091
Which category of Maithil Brahmins emerged as the highest tier 
during the later development of the Panji system?
Shrotriya
18092
The categorisation of Brahmins into 'Panch-Gauda' and 'Panch-
Dravida' uses which mountain range as the geographical boundary?
Vindhya Range
18093
Under which Maharaja's reign was the 'Shakha Panji' (branch 
records) formally introduced?
Maharaja Madhava Singh
18094
What does the 'Laukik' refer to in the context of Shrotriya Panji 
management?
Specific social or lineage-based characteristics unique to the 
Shrotriya group.
18095
In the Panji terminology, families that were not formally registered 
but were otherwise eligible are known as _____.
Jaibar
18096
Which philosopher's 'Tantra Vartika' is cited as an early reference 
for the practice of 'Samuh-Lekhya' (group records)?
Kumarila Bhatta

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18097
What is the primary difference between Chandoga and Vajasaneyi 
marriage rituals regarding timing?
Chandoga marriages have a specific 'Uttara Vivaha' performed after 
sunset after seeing the Arundhati star.
18098
In 'Chhadwith' calculations, the first relation checked (the paternal 
great-grandfather's father) is known as the _____.
First Chhadwith
18099
Concept: Sapanait
Definition: The condition of being related within prohibited degrees 
of kinship, making marriage illegal under local custom.
18100
How many 'Shakha' (branches) are documented for the specific 
Mandar Mool of the Kashyap Gotra?
74
18101
Which Gotra is exclusively found among the Karn Kayasthas of 
Mithila according to the source?
Kashyap
18102
What is the significance of the year 1019 Shaka mentioned in the 
Sanskrit verse regarding King Nanyadeva?
It marks the establishment of the Karnat dynasty in Mithila.
18103
The 'Uderh' document for a groom traditionally lists _____ 
ancestors.
32
18104
Which Panji records 'Kshatran' (flaws) to ensure descendants are 
aware of ancestral history?
Dooshan Panji
18105
What does the 'Mool Panji' primarily record?
The original ancestors (Beeji Purush) and their primary ancestral 
villages.
18106
Maithil Brahmins are classified into how many major social tiers 
(such as Shrotriya and Yogya)?
Three
18107
Which type of Panji helps in identifying 'Sapinda' relationships 
(close blood relations)?
Uderh Panji
18108
True or False: According to the Panji rules, a person can marry 
within their own Gotra if the Mool is different.
False
18109
Which season or month is specifically mentioned as the time to dry 
and preserve Panji books from moisture?
When the Sun is in the Leo (Simha) constellation (approx. 
Augustâ€“September).
18110
What is the 16th Chhadwith relation used in marriage verification?
The maternal grandmother's maternal grandmother's father.
18111
The scholar who held the title 'Panji-Shastra Martand' was _____.
Modanand Jha
18112
Which family lineage is identified as having provided the majority 
of the original Panjikars?
The family of Gunakar Jha (Mandar Mool).
18113
The classification of Maithil Brahmins into Shrotriya, Yogya, and 
Panjibaddha occurred during the _____ period.
Medieval (Post-Karnat)
18114
How did the Panji system affect the Karn Kayastha community 
compared to Brahmins?
It followed similar genealogical principles but focused on different 
Mools and a single Gotra (Kashyap).
18115
Which historical source is used to determine the Pravaras of a 
specific Gotra?
The Vedic Samhitas (specifically the Rigveda).
18116
What was the purpose of creating 'Asvajanpatra'?
To serve as formal proof that the bride and groom do not share 
common blood within prohibited degrees.
18117
Why is the Mithila Panji system compared to 'Genome Mapping' in 
the text's title?
Because it tracks hereditary data and genetic lineage with precision 
over 1,500 years.
18118
In the context of the Panji, what is the 'Panch-Gauda' group?
The five northern Brahmin communities: Utkala, Kanyakubja, 
Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
18119
Who was the ruler of Mithila during the time Maharaja Mahesh 
Thakur provided revised marriage guidelines?
The source suggests he was a pivotal scholar-ruler during the 
Mughal era (Maharaja of Darbhanga).
18120
Which relation is specifically excluded in Rule 4 of marriage 
eligibility?
The children of the groom's step-mother's brother.
18121
What is the traditional definition of 'Swa-jana' in the Panji system?
A person with whom marriage is prohibited due to close blood 
relationship.
18122
Which town served as the capital of the ancient Videha kingdom, 
which later became the heartland of the Panji system?
Mithila
18123
The 9th Chhadwith relation corresponds to the bride's _____.
Maternal grandfather's paternal great-grandfather.
18124
Which tier of Brahmins are referred to as 'Pachhwari-paat'?
Non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddha).
18125
The 'Gotra Panji' contains a comprehensive list of _____.
All Gotras and their associated ancient Mools.
18126
Which Panji expert was examined orally by Sir Ganganath Jha?
Modanand Jha
18127
The record that tracks the migration and current residence of family 
members is the _____.
Patra Panji
18128
How many Pravaras are assigned to the Sandilya Gotra?
Three (Sandilya, Asita, and Devala).
18129
How many generations of the maternal side are checked if the bride 
and groom belong to the same Mool?
Five
18130
Which relation constitutes the 'Third Chhadwith'?
The bride's paternal grandfather's maternal grandfather's father.
18131
What historical role did the 'Paricheta' play in the 14th century?
They were officials who travelled from village to village to collect 
and verify family genealogical data.
18132
The term for a marriage that violates the Panji rules is _____.
Ashastriya (or Sapanait marriage).
18133
Which document is considered the 'Lifeblood' (Pranadhara) of the 
Tirhuta script today?
The Panji Prabandh records.
18134
What was the primary administrative purpose of the Panji Prabandh 
in Mithila?
To maintain caste purity among Brahmins, Kayasthas, and 
Kshatriyas through genealogical records.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18135
The formal pedigree or introduction of 32 ancestral lineages 
required for marriage eligibility is known as _____.
Udegh
18136
How many distinct Gotras are identified within the Maithil Brahmin 
community according to the Panji records?
20
18137
Which historical ruler of the Karnata dynasty is credited with 
institutionalising the Panji system in $1326$ AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva
18138
What is the equivalent year in the Shaka calendar for the 
establishment of the Panji Prabandh in $1326$ AD?
Saka $1248$
18139
In the Panji hierarchy, what are the three primary categories of 
Brahmins?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
18140
What term was used to describe those Brahmins who were not 
formally recorded in the Panji registers?
Jaibar
18141
For the Shrotriya class, how many generations must be excluded for 
marriage on the paternal side?
Seven generations.
18142
For the Shrotriya class, how many generations must be excluded for 
marriage on the maternal side?
Five generations.
18143
According to the Panji rules for the Panjibaddh class, how many 
paternal generations must be avoided for marriage?
Six generations.
18144
How many total 'Mools' (original ancestral roots) of Brahmins are 
identified in Mithila?
$167$
18145
Out of the $167$ Mools, how many are considered the primary or 
main Mools?
$34$ (later $35$ with the addition of the Khana branch).
18146
A Brahmin with three ancestors who authored Vedic hymns is 
classified as having a _____.
Tri-pravara
18147
A Brahmin with five ancestors who authored Vedic hymns is 
classified as having a _____.
Panch-pravara
18148
Why is marriage prohibited between individuals of the Savarna and 
Vatsa Gotras?
Because they share the same common ancestors.
18149
Name the five divisions of the 'Pancha Gauda' Brahmins.
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
18150
The geographical boundary for the Pancha Gauda Brahmins is the 
region north of the _____ Mountains.
Vindhyachal
18151
Maithil Brahmins following the Madhyandina branch of the 
Yajurveda are known as _____.
Vajasaneyi
18152
Maithil Brahmins following the Kauthuma branch of the Samaveda 
are known as _____.
Chandogya
18153
What is the key difference in wedding timing between Chandogya 
and Vajasaneyi Brahmins?
Chandogya marriages occur after sunset following the sighting of 
the star Arundhati.
18154
Which philosopher's 'Tantra Vartika' contains the earliest mention 
of the 'Samuh Lekhya' (group records) used in genealogy?
Kumarila Bhatta
18155
What specific document must a Panjikar issue to certify that a bride 
and groom are not related (non-Sapinda)?
Siddhanta Patra (also called Asvajana Patra).
18156
Who was the first Panjikar appointed for the Brahmin community 
by Maharaja Harisimhadeva?
Gunakar Jha
18157
What is the term for the original patriarch or ancestor who defines a 
lineage in the Panji records?
Biji Purusha
18158
What defines the 'Mool Gram' of an individual in the Panji system?
The original ancestral village from which the lineage began.
18159
How many Gotras are found within the Karna Kayastha community 
of Mithila?
One (Kashyapa).
18160
Term: Sakha Panji
Definition: A branch-wise genealogical record introduced during 
the time of Maharaja Madhav Singh to further categorise Brahmin 
sub-groups.
18161
In which script are the traditional Panji records of Mithila written?
Tirhuta (also known as Mithilakshara).
18162
What specific month is recommended for drying and maintaining 
the palm-leaf (Talapatra) Panji records?
The month when the Sun is in the zodiac sign of Leo (Simbha), 
roughly 16 August to 15 September.
18163
What is the minimum number of generations on the maternal side 
that must be cleared to satisfy 'Sapinda' rules according to Manu?
Five generations.
18164
What is the historical significance of the 'Smritisara' author Hari 
Nath Upadhyaya in Panji history?
He mistakenly married a close relative due to a lack of genealogical 
records, prompting the formalisation of the Panji Prabandh.
18165
The prohibition of marrying the daughter of a mother's brother or a 
father's sister is a core rule of the _____ system.
Panji
18166
What does the term 'Laukika' refer to in the context of the Shrotriya 
Panji?
The specific hierarchical status and marriage alliances tracked for 
the Shrotriya class.
18167
How many 'Chauris' (grades/classes) exist within the Shrotriya 
Panji records?
Eight
18168
The classification of Brahmins as 'Pachhwari-paat' generally refers 
to which group?
The non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddh).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18169
What happens to the hierarchical status of a lineage in the Shrotriya 
class over time due to inferior marriage alliances?
The status is 'Adhogami' (descending) and can eventually lead to 
the loss of Shrotriya ranking.
18170
What is the specific prohibition regarding the 'Matrimah' (maternal 
grandmother's) lineage in Udegh?
Marriage is prohibited if the groom and bride are within six 
generations of a common ancestor in that lineage.
18171
Which Brahmin Gotra is associated with the Pravaras: Shandilya, 
Asit, and Deval?
Shandilya
18172
Which Brahmin Gotra is associated with the Pravaras: Bhargava, 
Jamadagnya, Aurva, Chyavana, and Apnuvana?
Vatsa (or Savarna).
18173
The term 'Mool' refers to the original root, while the village of 
current residence is known as _____.
Dera
18174
Who is the legendary Biji Purusha of the lineage starting with $1/1$ 
in the Panji records?
Gangadhar of the Gangoli Mool.
18175
Which Mool is associated with the scholar Vidyapati Thakur?
Bisphi
18176
According to the excerpts, why is the Panji system considered a 
'Genome Map' of the Mithila region?
Because it tracks hereditary traits, lineages, and genetic purity over 
more than a millennium.
18177
What is the status of the Panji system among the Mithila Kshatriya 
community today?
It has largely become extinct over time.
18178
The preservation rule 'Jalat Rakshet, Tailat Rakshet' advises keeping 
Panji books away from which two substances?
Water and Oil.
18179
In the table provided, the lineage of 'Ayabhata' ($27$) traces back 
to which Mool and Biji Purusha?
Mandai Mool and Biji Purusha Trivikram Bhatta.
18180
What was the 'Asvajan' status awarded to Siddhanta Patra 
applicants?
A declaration that the two families are unrelated within the 
prohibited degrees.
18181
How many ancestors are included in the identification of 'Chhaid-
vadh' (the six-step lineage check)?
Six generations of ancestors in both paternal and maternal lines.
18182
Identify the Gotra associated with the Mool 'Khauaay'.
Kashyapa
18183
Identify the Gotra associated with the Mool 'Ekhatta'.
Bharadwaj
18184
Which Mool belongs to the Katyayana Gotra?
Kujoli
18185
The term 'Mula-Brahmapura' can be found across how many 
different Gotras?
Six (Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, Alambukaaksh, Gautama, and 
Gargya).
18186
In the context of the Panji, what does the title 'Mahamahopadhyaya' 
signify?
A high academic/scholarly title granted to learned individuals 
within a lineage.
18187
The person who officially calculates and records these lineages is 
called a _____.
Panjikar
18188
What is the significance of the year $2009$ AD in the title of the 
provided document?
It marks the end date of the chronological period covered by the 
research ($450$ AD to $2009$ AD).
18189
Which Mool is associated with the scholar and author of 'Varna 
Ratnakara', Jyotirishwar Thakur?
Pallie
18190
The rule 'Matritah Panchami Tyaktwa Pitritah Saptami Bhajet' 
refers to avoiding _____ generations on the mother's side and 
_____ on the father's.
Five; Seven.
18191
What was the role of 'Ashta Shrotriya' in the social hierarchy of 
Mithila?
They represented the eight highest grades of genealogical purity 
within the Shrotriya class.
18192
Which specific village is linked to the Mool 'Ghusout'?
Trilathi
18193
The concept of 'Biji' essentially serves as the _____ of a lineage in 
modern genomic terms.
Founder or root ancestor.
18194
According to the Panji excerpts, what determines one's membership 
in a specific Gotra?
Paternal descent from an ancient Sage (Rishi).
18195
What unique genealogical challenge is faced by the Karna Kayastha 
community regarding Gotras?
They all belong to a single Gotra (Kashyapa), so marriage is 
determined solely by 'Mool' (ancestral village) identity.
18196
The term 'Siddhant' in Mithila marriage customs refers to the formal 
_____.
Permission or certification for marriage by a Panjikar.
18197
Why is the preservation of Tirhuta script considered vital for Panji 
management?
Because almost all historical genealogical records are written 
exclusively in this script.
18198
According to the text, which class of Brahmins is designated as 
'Sva-jan' in ancestral records?
Those who share common ancestors within the prohibited degree of 
seven generations.
18199
The Panji records identify how many primary classes or categories 
for the Shrotriya class?
Eight (known as Chauris).
18200
Which script-preservation project is mentioned as starting in the 
year $2000$ AD?
The transliteration of Tirhuta Panji records into Devanagari.
18201
Which Gotra includes the Mools: Onini, Sanaki-dhi, and Mandan?
Kashyapa
18202
What was the significance of 'Upar-Teep' in Panji documentation?
It refers to supplemental genealogical notes or branch updates 
within the Shrotriya records.
18203
The term 'Adau' in Panji entries refers to the _____.
Original or primary entry point of a lineage.
18204
Who is the primary author/researcher of the research titled 'Genome 
Mapping: Mithilak Panji Prabandh'?
Gajendra Thakur

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18205
What historical event in $1326$ AD led to the creation of the Panji 
registers?
The realisation that learned men were unknowingly entering into 
prohibited marriages due to forgotten lineages.
18206
During which historical period was the Panji Prabandh system 
established?
The late medieval period.
18207
Define the term 'Uterh' in the context of Maithil genealogy.
An introduction or pedigree identifying seven generations of 
ancestors to determine marriage eligibility.
18208
How many ancestral lineages ('kuls') are required to establish a 
complete 'Uterh'?
Thirty-two.
18209
According to the Panji system, individuals who were not formally 
registered were categorised as _____.
Jayabaar
18210
For Shrotriya Brahmins, how many generations must be excluded 
on the father's side for a marriage to be valid?
Seven.
18211
For Shrotriya Brahmins, how many generations must be excluded 
on the mother's side for a marriage to be valid?
Five.
18212
The individuals known as 'Panjibadh' are generally required to 
avoid _____ generations on the father's side.
Six
18213
How many types of 'Gotras' are identified in the Maithil Brahmin 
community?
Twenty.
18214
How many 'Mools' (original roots) were initially identified in the 
Panji system?
One hundred and sixty-seven.
18215
Which two specific Vedic branches do Maithil Brahmins typically 
follow?
Samavedi and Shukla Yajurvedi.
18216
Maithil Brahmins following the Samaveda (Kauthuma branch) are 
referred to as _____.
Chandogya
18217
Maithil Brahmins following the Shukla Yajurveda (Madhyandini 
branch) are referred to as _____.
Vajasaneyi
18218
How many 'Mools' are considered primary out of the total 167?
Thirty-four (later thirty-five including the Kharnam branch).
18219
What are the three main hierarchical categories of Maithil Brahmins 
mentioned in the text?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibadh.
18220
List the three Gotras that belong to the 'Pancha-Pravara' category.
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaj (specifically listed as Vatsa, 
Savarna, and Garga in the Pravara section).
18221
In Maithil tradition, a 'Tri-Pravara' Gotra is one whose ancestors 
composed three _____ of the Rigveda.
Suktas (hymns)
18222
Which King is credited with the formal systematisation of the Panji 
records in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
18223
The specific date for Maharaja Harisimhadeva's decree is Shaka 
1248, which corresponds to the year _____ AD.
1326
18224
Who was the scholar appointed by Harisimhadeva to manage the 
Brahmin Panji records?
Pandit Gunakar Jha.
18225
Which scholar was appointed to manage the Panji records for the 
Karna Kayasthas?
Shankar Dutt.
18226
Who was appointed as the registrar for the Kshatriya Panji records 
under Harisimhadeva?
Vijay Dutt.
18227
The certificate of marriage compatibility issued by a Panjikar is 
known as _____.
Siddhant Patra (or Aswajanchan Patra)
18228
What is the meaning of 'Pancha-Gauda' in the context of Brahmin 
classification?
The five northern Brahmin groups: Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, 
Maithil, and Saraswat.
18229
Which mountain range serves as the geographical boundary 
between the Pancha-Gauda and Pancha-Dravida Brahmins?
The Vindhya mountains.
18230
Define 'Biji Purusha' in Maithil genealogy.
The earliest known ancestor or the progenitor of a specific 
lineage/Mool.
18231
What is a 'Moolgram'?
The original ancestral village from which a family lineage 
originated.
18232
The Shrotriya category is further divided into _____ classes or 
'Chaurallas'.
Eight
18233
How many 'Panjis' (books of records) currently exist for the 
Shrotriya category?
Thirty-two.
18234
The 'Pachhwariapaar' group refers to Brahmins other than the 
_____ category.
Shrotriya
18235
What is the purpose of 'Dushan Panji'?
To record 'flaws' or deviations in lineage, such as inter-caste 
marriages or unconventional unions, to warn future generations.
18236
A marriage is prohibited if the bride is a 'Sapinda' of the groom's 
mother within _____ generations.
Five.
18237
A marriage is prohibited if the bride is a 'Sapinda' of the groom's 
father within _____ generations.
Seven.
18238
Which script is traditionally used to write and maintain the Panji 
records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshara).
18239
How many Gotras are considered highly organised and numerous in 
the Maithil Brahmin community?
Seven.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18240
Term: 'Asvajanchan Patra'
Definition: A document confirming that the bride and groom are 
not closely related by blood (not 'Svajana').
18241
In the Karna Kayastha community, only one Gotra is typically 
followed, which is _____.
Kashyap
18242
Under which Maharaja did the 'Sakha Panji' and the rigid hierarchy 
of Shrotriya and other classes begin?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
18243
According to the text, what happened to the Panji system among the 
Kshatriya community over time?
It largely disappeared, and they now follow the rule of avoiding the 
same village for marriage.
18244
What is the mnemonic rule for preserving Panji books from 
physical damage?
Protect them from water, oil, and loose binding ('Jalat Raksha, 
Tailat Raksha...').
18245
How many specific 'Chhattha' (relationship check-points) are used 
to determine marriage eligibility?
Sixteen.
18246
The 'Tri-Pravara' for the Shandilya Gotra consists of Shandilya, 
Asit, and _____.
Deval
18247
Which Gotra includes ancestors such as Shakti, Vashistha, and 
Parashar in its Pravara?
Parashar.
18248
The 'Pancha-Pravara' for the Vatsa Gotra includes Orva, Chyavana, 
Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and _____.
Apnuvana
18249
What does the 'Laukik' refer to in the context of Shrotriya 
genealogy?
The specific lineage branch or record book of the Shrotriya 
Brahmins.
18250
Which ancestral village is associated with the 'Oini' Mool of the 
Kashyap Gotra?
Oini village (associated with the Oinwar dynasty).
18251
The term 'Maddhyam Mool' refers to the _____ category of lineages 
in the Panji system.
Intermediate
18252
According to the 'Tantra Varttik' by Kumarila Bhatta, why were 
'Samuha-Lekhya' (group records) started?
To ensure high-born families did not forget their ancestral traditions 
and to track virtues and flaws in lineages.
18253
In which century did Kumarila Bhatta live, providing early evidence 
of group lineage records?
The 7th Century AD.
18254
Which specific ancestor is the 'Biji Purusha' of the Mandar Mool, 
dating back to approximately 400 AD?
Mahamahopadhyaya Trivenibhatta.
18255
What religious text serves as the foundation for the marriage rules 
implemented in the Panji system?
The Smritis (specifically Manu Smriti and Yajnavalkya Smriti).
18256
The term 'Genome Mapping' is used in the text as a modern 
metaphor for which ancient system?
The Panji Prabandh (Maithil genealogical records).
18257
Which Maithil Maharaja requested the writing of the 'Sakha 
Pranayan' book?
Maharaja Madhav Singh of Darbhanga.
18258
A marriage between the daughter of a maternal uncle and the son of 
a paternal aunt is generally _____ under Panji rules.
Prohibited
18259
What is the role of 'Mool' and 'Sakh' in identifying a Maithil 
Brahmin?
It identifies their original root lineage and their current family 
branch.
18260
Which poet's 19-generation lineage is meticulously preserved in the 
Sakha Panji?
Vidyapati Thakur.
18261
The text mentions that even some Kayasthas and Kshatriyas had 
Panji systems. To which Kayastha sub-group does this specifically 
apply?
Karna Kayasthas.
18262
Which branch of Brahmins performs the 'Upanayana' (sacred thread 
ceremony) with four specific Sanskaras?
Chandoga (Samavedi) and Vajasaneyi (Yajurvedi) have slight 
variations in these rituals.
18263
For a 'Vajasaneyi' marriage, is there a specific requirement to see 
the Arundhati star?
No, that requirement is specific to the 'Chandoga' (Samavedi) 
marriage ritual.
18264
The 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins belong to the regions south of the 
_____.
Vindhya mountains
18265
List the five Dravida Brahmin groups.
Karnataka, Gurjara, Tailanga, Dravida, and Maharashtriya.
18266
The record-keeping books were traditionally bound using wooden 
planks and a thick cotton thread called _____.
Sutari
18267
What was the significance of the 'Saka 1019' date in the text?
It marks the arrival of King Nanyadeva and the establishment of the 
Karnat dynasty in Mithila.
18268
The genealogical record of a person who married a 'Dasa' (servant) 
or someone from a lower caste was moved to the _____.
Dushan Panji
18269
According to the text, a child born from a marriage with a 'Svajana' 
(close relative) was traditionally considered a _____.
Chandala (outcaste)
18270
Which scholar's lineage is traced for 18 generations up to the 
registrar Modanand Jha?
Pandit Gunakar Jha.
18271
The practice of 'Siddhant' requires the groom and bride's families to 
meet the _____ before the wedding.
Panjikar
18272
How does the Panji system handle 'Moolgram' changes due to 
migration?
The original village name is retained as the 'Mool', but the new 
village is added as the current residence.
18273
Which Gotra has the highest number of branches (74) identified in 
Mithila?
Kashyap.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18274
What is 'Apanhi' or 'Yaatni' à¤ªà¤°à¤¿à¤šà¤¯ ?
The self-maintained family record used before the formal Panji 
Prabandh was established.
18275
The Sanskrit phrase 'Matritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami' refers to 
avoiding _____.
Five maternal generations and seven paternal generations in 
marriage.
18276
Who provided the 'Samprami Tyajet' (7-generation rule) ruling that 
became standard for Maithil society?
Mahamahopadhyaya Mahesh Thakur.
18277
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Kujouli' and 'Jalki' Mools?
Katyayan.
18278
True or False: The Panji system allows marriage if the couple shares 
the same Pravara but different Gotras.
False.
18279
The historical figure Aryabhata is recorded in the Mandar Mool of 
the _____ Gotra.
Kashyap
18280
Which branch of Samavedi Brahmins is Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's 
teacher, Ilamapati Upadhyaya, associated with?
Karah-Bisai branch of the Vatsa Gotra.
18281
What title was given to Modanand Jha by Maharaja Kameshwar 
Singh for his expertise in Panji?
Panji Shastra Martand (or Labdh-Dhout).
18282
The 'Pancha Dravida' classification includes the Brahmins of 
Gujarat and _____.
Maharashtra
18283
In the genealogical tables, what does the term 'Agnyat' 
(à¤…à¤œà¥à¤žà¤¾à¤¤) signify?
An unknown lineage, often leading to a 'Dushan' (flaw) in the 
record.
18284
List the seven distinct types of Panji (genealogical records) found in 
Mithila.
Mool, Shakha, Gotra, Patra, Dooshan, Utredh, and Aswajanyapatra.
18285
Which specific Panji is used to record the 'ksharan' (erosion or 
flaws) within a lineage?
The Dooshan Panji.
18286
What was the primary purpose of the 'Shakha Panji' commissioned 
by Maharaja Madhav Singh?
To distinguish between the categories of Sochhat, Jog, and 
Panjibaddha.
18287
In Mithila genealogy, what information is uniquely preserved in the 
'Gotra Panji'?
All the Gotras and their ancient 'Mools' (original village roots).
18288
The 'Patra Panji' came into common usage approximately how 
many years ago?
500 years.
18289
Which Panji type provides knowledge regarding 'Sahapindak 
Nivritti' and mentions ancestors?
The Utredh Panji.
18290
What unique feature distinguishes the Rasaá¸ h-Arariya Panji from 
all others?
It includes the names of females (Streegana).
18291
In what year and month did the modern transcription and 
transliteration of ancient Panji records begin?
Chaitra of 2000 CE.
18292
How long has the genealogical history of the people of Mithila been 
documented according to the records?
Over 1500 years.
18293
Who is the historical king of the Karnat dynasty credited as the 
official founder of the Panji-Prabandha?
Maharaja Harasimhadeva.
18294
Which scholar was appointed as the first Panjikar for the Maithil 
Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
18295
In the context of the Panji system, who was the first Panjikar for the 
Karna Kayasthas?
Shankardatt.
18296
The current form of the Panji-Prabandha was formally initiated in 
which Shaka year?
1248 Shaka (1326 AD).
18297
Around what year did the 'Shrotriya' sub-caste emerge in British 
India as a distinct group within Maithil Brahmins?
1800 CE.
18298
Which modern scholar provided the digitized copies of genealogical 
records to Anshuman Pandey in 2007?
Gajendra Thakur.
18299
In which region of Mithila does the 'Shrotriya' sub-caste still NOT 
exist today?
Nepal.
18300
According to the Dooshan Panji, how many generations of marriage 
into pure lineages are required to achieve the status of 'Purnatah 
Shuddha'?
Six generations.
18301
Gangesha Upadhyaya, the founder of Navya-Nyaya, is associated 
with which specific 'Mool' and 'Gram'?
Chhadan.
18302
What significant personal detail about Gangesha Upadhyaya's birth 
is recorded in the Dooshan Panji?
He was born five years after the passing of his father (Pitri 
Parokshe).
18303
According to the ancient genealogical scrolls, what was the social 
status of Gangesha Upadhyaya's wife, Medha?
She belonged to the 'Charmkarini' (tanner) community.
18304
What academic titles were held by Gangesha Upadhyaya that 
denoted his supreme status as a teacher?
Jagadguru and Paramguru.
18305
Besides Gangesha Upadhyaya, which other scholar was later 
conferred the title of 'Paramguru'?
Nutan Vachaspati (successor of Vriddha Vachaspati).
18306
Who is the author of the 1958 work 'History of Navya-Nyaya in 
Mithila'?
Dinesh Chandra Bhattacharya.
18307
Which modern scholar is accused in the text of 'honour killing' 
Gangesha Upadhyaya's history by hiding the inter-caste marriage 
details?
Prof. Ramanath Jha.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18308
What is the name of the famous Nyaya author who was the son of 
Gangesha Upadhyaya?
Vardhamana.
18309
Which scholar completed the first full English translation of all four 
volumes of the 'Tattvachintamani'?
Stephen Phillips.
18310
List the four volumes (Khandas) that comprise the 
'Tattvachintamani'.
Pratyaksha, Anumana, Upamana, and Shabda.
18311
Which publishing house produced V.P. Bhatta's translations of the 
'Tattvachintamani'?
Eastern Book Linkers, Delhi.
18312
Who wrote the monograph on Gangesha Upadhyaya for the Sahitya 
Akademi that the text critiques for inaccuracies?
Udayanath Jha 'Ashok'.
18313
In the Karnat dynasty, who was the founder that established the 
lineage in 1019 Shaka?
Nanyadeva.
18314
Which ruler of Mithila was defeated by Ghiyasuddin Tughlaq in 
1324-25 AD?
Maharaja Harasimhadeva.
18315
What was the original meaning of the term 'Shrotriya' before it 
became a sub-caste in 1800 CE?
It referred to a specific stream of Vedic education.
18316
According to the Dooshan Panji, which descendant in the lineage of 
Gunawati Himsha was the first to be considered 'completely pure'?
Devadhar.
18317
Which Bengal scholar passed the 'Shalaka' examination in Mithila 
to receive the title of 'Sarvabhaum'?
Vasudeva Sarvabhauma.
18318
Why did the scholars of Mithila prohibit the copying of the 
'Tattvachintamani'?
To maintain intellectual control and force students to memorise the 
text.
18319
Which scholar established the Navya-Nyaya school in Navadvipa 
after defeating Pakshadhar Mishra?
Raghunath Shiromani.
18320
The title 'Sukavikairavakananenduh' used for Gangesha by his son 
implies expertise in what area besides logic?
Poetry (Kavya).
18321
What does the text claim was the 'revenge of nature' against Mithila 
for suppressing Gangesha's family history?
The extinction of the Navya-Nyaya school from the region.
18322
In the example from Table 1-97, who was the woman from a 
different caste whose marriage to Chamaru began a specific lineage 
flaw?
Gunawati Himsha.
18323
Which Panji root (Mool) is associated with the marriage of 
Gangesha's daughter to the son of Jayaditya?
Chhadan.
18324
What was the name of the daughter of the Koch Rajvanshi king 
who married into the Maithil community?
Sohago.
18325
According to the Devanand Panji 30-5, which son of Gangesha was 
an 'Upayakara' and Mahamahopadhyaya?
Vardhamana.
18326
In the Dooshan Panji, what does the term 'Agnat' signify when 
placed in a genealogical table?
That the lineage, caste, or maternal details (Matrik) are unknown.
18327
Who was the maternal grandfather (Matrik) of Gangadhar in the 
lineage of Gunawati Himsha?
Bhavnath.
18328
What was the name of the son born to Rud (Rudra) and the 
daughter of Kanhu in the Darihara lineage example?
Chand.
18329
Which specific village is mentioned as the 'Mool' for the scholar 
Udayanacharya?
Nanauti (Nanautivar Mool).
18330
According to the records, the 'Shrotriya' sub-caste was created 
based on orders for 'upgradation' in exchange for _____.
Cash (monetary payments).
18331
Which logic volume of the Tattvachintamani deals with 
'Comparison'?
Upamana Khanda.
18332
What modern title is equated to the historic academic rank of 
'Jagadguru'?
Distinguished University Professor.
18333
The Tattvachintamani translation of 'Inference of God' was 
published in which year by V.P. Bhatta?
2025
18334
In the context of the Panji records, what is a 'Panjikar'?
A traditional genealogist who maintains and updates the lineage 
scrolls.
18335
Which Panji document is referred to in the text as the 'Blackbook'?
The Dooshan Panji.
18336
What is the name of the literary work where Maharaja 
Harasimhadeva is portrayed as the hero by Jyotirishwar Thakur?
Varna-Ratnakara.
18337
Which Panjikar was appointed for the Kshatriya community by 
Maharaja Harasimhadeva?
Vijaydatt.
18338
What was the date of Maharaja Harasimhadeva's birth?
1294 AD.
18339
In what year did Maharaja Harasimhadeva ascend the throne?
1307 AD.
18340
Which scholar is credited with providing the first full English 
translation of the Shabda volume of Tattvachintamani?
Stephen Phillips.
18341
The transcription of ancient Panjis into modern scripts aimed to 
reveal Mithila's _____ diversity.
Cultural.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18342
According to the Panji classification, which type deal specifically 
with the original villages of lineages?
Mool Panji.
18343
In the Dooshan Panji examples, what other common community 
name (besides Charmkar) appears as an 'inter-caste' flaw?
Rajak (Washerman).
18344
Who was the disciple of Vasudeva Sarvabhauma that memorised 
the Tattvachintamani to take it to Bengal?
Raghunath Shiromani.
18345
The text describes the emergence of the Shrotriya sub-caste as a 
result of influence on which Maharaja?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
18346
According to the table in Record 1, who was the son of Gangadhar? Visho.
18347
What was the name of the son of Visho mentioned in the Sakaradhi 
Mool example?
Goge.
18348
Who was the father of Rud (Rudra) in the genealogical table 
example?
Kanhu.
18349
In the Dooshan Panji, which character's daughter married Horai of 
the Pali Mool?
Chand.
18350
The Tattvachintamani is described as being equivalent to how many 
other texts?
12000
18351
Which famous Maithil poet was a contemporary of Pakshadhar 
Mishra?
Vidyapati.
18352
What specific term describes a lineage that has been completely 
purged of its recorded flaws?
Purnatah Shuddha.
18353
Which Panji type includes the knowledge of ancestors and 
'Sahapindak' status?
Utredh Panji.
18354
The term 'Moolgram' became a standard inclusion in which type of 
Panji roughly 500 years ago?
Patra Panji.
18355
In the Karnat dynasty, the Shaka year 1019 is equivalent to what 
year in the Common Era?
1097 AD.
18356
What was the title of the dissertation submitted to the University of 
Michigan regarding Maithil Brahmin caste identity?
Recasting the Brahmin in Medieval Mithila.
18357
Which Panjikar families are noted for being reluctant to share their 
records as 'intellectual property'?
The traditional Panjikars of Mithila.
18358
What is the name of the website where Panji Manuscripts were 
uploaded in 2009?
Videha Pothi (www.videha.co.in).
18359
According to the Dooshan Panji, the son of Gangesha named 
'Supan' had a grandson named _____.
BhaveÅ›var.
18360
Which Panji type specifically deals with the branching out of 
families from their original roots?
Shakha Panji.
18361
Who was the Panjikar that held the records of the Maithil Brahmins 
during the 14th century reform?
Gunakar Jha.
18362
In the provided text, what does 'Aswajanyapatra' represent in the 
classification of seven Panjis?
Records of unrelated or non-hereditary lineages.
18363
Which scholar's 'wry gaze' or 'crooked look' did the people of 
Mithila have to endure according to the text?
The contemporary rulers ( à¤¶à¤¾à¤¸à¤•à¤• à¤µà¤•à¥ à¤° 
à¤¦à¥ƒà¤›à¤¿).
18364
The translation of the 'Pratyaksha' volume of Tattvachintamani by 
V.P. Bhatta was published in what year?
2012
18365
According to the text, the poems of Gangesha Upadhyaya are 
currently _____.
Unavailable (due to a conspiracy of suppression).
18366
In the genealogical tables, what does the number '178/2' or '188/2' 
typically refer to?
Specific page or entry numbers within the Panji volumes.
18367
Which logic volume deals with 'Verbal Testimony' or 'Word'?
Shabda Khanda.
18368
What is the title of the 2020 Bloomsbury Academic translation of 
the Tattvachintamani?
Jewel of Reflection on the Truth about Epistemology.
18369
What is the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' in Maithil 
society?
To maintain genealogical records and ensure caste purity for 
marriage eligibility.
18370
Which three social groups were originally included in the 'Panji 
Prabandh' record-keeping system?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
18371
In the context of Panji, what does the term 'Udeht' represent?
A formal introduction identifying seven generations of an 
individual's ancestors.
18372
How many total ancestors' introductions are ideally required to 
establish a full genealogical identity in the Panji system?
Thirty-two (32).
18373
To be eligible for marriage, a Maithil Brahmin must typically avoid 
common ancestry for how many generations on the paternal side?
Seven generations.
18374
According to the Panji rules, how many generations must be 
avoided on the maternal side for marriage eligibility?
Five generations.
18375
The categories of Maithil Brahmins resulting from the Panji 
classification are Shrotriya, Yogya, and _____.
Panjibaddh

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18376
What is the term for those Brahmins who did not register in the 
Panji records?
Jaywar (Jaebat).
18377
How many total 'Gotras' are recognized within the Maithil Brahmin 
community?
Twenty (20).
18378
How many primary 'Moolgrams' (root villages) were originally 
identified in the Maithil Panji?
One hundred and sixty-seven (167).
18379
In the Maithil Brahmin hierarchy, how many 'Mools' are considered 
the main or primary ones?
Thirty-four (34), later expanded to thirty-five (35).
18380
The two main Vedic branches followed by Maithil Brahmins are 
the Samavedi (Chhandogya) and the _____.
Yajurvedi (Vajasaneyi)
18381
Which historical King of the Karnata dynasty initiated the formal 
'Panji Prabandh' in 1326 AD (Shake 1248)?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
18382
Who was appointed as the first official 'Panjikar' by King 
Harisimhadeva?
Gunakar Jha.
18383
What are the two types of 'Pravara' families identified among 
Maithil Brahmins based on Rigvedic sage ancestors?
Tri-Pravara (3 ancestors) and Pancha-Pravara (5 ancestors).
18384
List the three Gotras categorized as 'Pancha-Pravara'.
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaj (specifically listed as Vatsa, 
Savarna, and Garga in some contexts, but text specifies Vatsa, 
Savarna, and Garga/Vardhanya).
18385
Concept: Siddhanta Patra (Asvajanajanita Patra)
Definition: A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the 
bride and groom have no prohibited blood relation.
18386
What geographical feature traditionally divides the 'Panch Gaud' 
Brahmins from the 'Panch Dravid' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
18387
Maithil Brahmins belong to which of the five northern Brahmin 
divisions ('Panch Gaud')?
Maithil.
18388
In the Chhandogya (Samavedi) marriage tradition, what are the two 
distinct sections of the ceremony?
Purva-Vivah and Uttar-Vivah.
18389
What is the significance of the 16 'Chhatth' (avoidance points) in 
Panji analysis?
They are specific genealogical positions of ancestors used to detect 
prohibited overlaps in lineage.
18390
Which 7th-century philosopher mentioned 'Samuha Lekhya' (family 
records) as a way to preserve lineage purity?
Kumarila Bhatta.
18391
Maithil Brahmins of the Chhandogya branch belong to the _____ 
Shaka of the Samaveda.
Kauthuma
18392
Maithil Brahmins of the Vajasaneyi branch follow the _____ Shaka 
of the Shukla Yajurveda.
Madhyandina
18393
What is the function of the 'Dushan Panji'?
To record flaws, lapses, or exceptions in a lineage to warn future 
generations.
18394
According to the texts, marriage is forbidden if the bride is the 
offspring of the groom's _____.
Maternal uncle (specifically 'Nimata' or mother's brother).
18395
Which specific Gotra is common to all Karna Kayasthas mentioned 
in the text?
Kashyap.
18396
How many 'Chhatth' (genealogical hurdles) must be checked for a 
bride to ensure she is not a 'Sapinda'?
Sixteen (16).
18397
The ancestors of the Savarna and Vatsa Gotras are considered the 
same, meaning marriage _____ occur between them.
Cannot (prohibited).
18398
What is the 'Laukik' in the context of Shrotriya Brahmins?
A lineage classification based on the social standing and marital 
history of their ancestors.
18399
Who revised the paternal avoidance rule from seven generations to 
six for the modern Maithil community?
Maharaja Mahesh Thakur.
18400
Which 'Mool' does the famous Maithili poet Vidyapati Thakur 
belong to?
Bisaphi (from the Kashyap Gotra).
18401
What does 'Panch-Dravid' refer to in the classification of Brahmins? The five southern Brahmin groups: Gurjar, Tailang, Dravid, Karnat, 
and Maharashtrian.
18402
What does the 'Shakha Panji' record?
Extended branch lineages, including titles, educational 
achievements, and migration details of individuals.
18403
The 'Panji Prabandh' system was established to prevent the status of 
_____ being conferred upon children of improper marriages.
Chandal
18404
What is the term for the maternal grandfather's lineage in the Udeht? Matamaha.
18405
What is the significance of Maharaja Madhav Singh's reign (c. 
1760 AD) in Panji history?
The introduction of 'Shakha Panji' and more rigid social sub-
divisions.
18406
Which Gotra includes ancestors such as Sandilya, Asit, and Deval?
Shandilya.
18407
Process: Determining Marriage Eligibility
Step: A Panjikar cross-references the 32 mools of the groom and 
bride to check for Sapinda (blood) overlaps within defined 
generation limits.
18408
The ancestors of the _____ Gotra include Shakti, Vashistha, and 
Parashar.
Parashar

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18409
Which Panjikar was awarded the title 'Panji Shastra Martand' by 
Maharaja Kameshwar Singh?
Modanand Jha.
18410
How many 'Paunjis' (record books) exist for the Shrotriya and 
Yogya/Panjibaddh categories respectively?
32 for Shrotriya and 153 for Yogya/Panjibaddh.
18411
The term 'Pachhwari-Paar' refers to which category of Maithil 
Brahmins?
Non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddh).
18412
According to Panji preservation rules, what should the books be 
protected from?
Water, oil, and loose binding.
18413
What is the recommended time of year to dry and air out Panji 
manuscripts?
When the Sun is in the Leo (Simha) constellation (approx. August-
September).
18414
Which Gotra lists 'Atri' and 'Sankriti' as two of its three Pravara 
ancestors?
Vashistha.
18415
The sage ancestors identified in the Pravara system are described as 
the original _____ of Rigvedic hymns.
Seers (or composers/Mantra-drashta).
18416
In the Panji system, what is a 'Mool' primarily based upon?
The ancestral village or place of origin.
18417
Identify the Gotra: Ancestors are Kashyap, Avatsar, and Naidhruvi.
Kashyap.
18418
Which historical era does the text mark as the beginning of the 
'Panji' record-keeping?
The transition to the Late Medieval period (approx. 13th-14th 
century).
18419
What is the 'Sapaninda' rule?
The prohibition of marriage between individuals sharing common 
ancestors within a certain number of degrees.
18420
True or False: The Panji system originally included records for 72 
Rajput clans.
True.
18421
Who was the Maithil King who appointed specific Panjikars for 
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas?
Harisimhadeva.
18422
The 'Gaur' Brahmins are located north of the _____ river/mountain 
range.
Vindhya
18423
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Ayan' (highest excellence) 
Mools like Kharaure and Khauare?
Shandilya (implied by lineage lists).
18424
The term 'Pubari-Paar' refers to which group in the Maithil social 
hierarchy?
Shrotriya Brahmins.
18425
Why is the maternal uncle's lineage ('Matri-paksha') strictly checked 
in Panji?
To ensure the groom is not marrying a girl from his mother's 
immediate family lineage up to the 5th generation.
18426
What is the 'Biji Purush' of the Mool 'Simbhuwal'?
Ratnapani.
18427
What was the primary purpose for the creation of the Panji 
Prabandh in Mithila?
To ensure the genealogical purity of Brahmin, Kayastha and 
Kshatriya castes for marriage purposes.
18428
According to the Panji Prabandh, how many generations of 
ancestors are included in the introduction known as 'Udegh'?
Seven generations.
18429
How many ancestors' introductions are required to establish an 
individual's 'Udegh'?
Thirty-two ancestors.
18430
Which three main castes were originally included in the Panji 
records during the medieval period?
Brahmin, Kayastha and Kshatriya.
18431
How many generations must be avoided for marriage on the father's 
side according to the 'Shrotriya' standards?
Seven generations.
18432
In the Shrotriya classification, how many generations must be 
avoided for marriage on the mother's side?
Five generations.
18433
What is the name for the category of individuals who were not 
included in the formal Panji records?
Jaybar (or Jaibar).
18434
The 'Panjibaddh' group of people are also referred to as _____.
Jaika Vanshaj.
18435
For the Panjibaddh category, how many generations must be 
avoided on the father's side for marriage?
Six generations.
18436
How many 'Gotras' (clans) are recognised within the Maithili 
Brahmin community?
Twenty.
18437
How many 'Moolgrams' (original villages) were initially identified 
in the Panji system?
One hundred and sixty-seven.
18438
Of the total Mools, how many were designated as the 'Mukhiya' 
(primary) Mools?
Thirty-four (later thirty-five).
18439
How many ancestors are cited in a 'Tri-pravara' lineage?
Three.
18440
How many ancestors are cited in a 'Pancha-pravara' lineage?
Five.
18441
Which two Gotras cannot intermarry because they share the same 
ancestors despite being separate clans?
Vatsa and Savarna.
18442
The 'Pancha Gauda' division of Brahmins includes Utkala, 
Kanyakubja, Gauda, Saraswat and _____.
Maithil.
18443
What geographical feature acts as the dividing line between 'Pancha 
Gauda' and 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal Mountains.
18444
Maithili Brahmins are followers of which two specific Vedas?
Yajurveda and Samaveda.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
18445
Followers of the Madhyandina branch of the Yajurveda in Mithila 
are called _____.
Vajasaneyi.
18446
Followers of the Kauthuma branch of the Samaveda in Mithila are 
called _____.
Chhandoga.
18447
What is the specific rule regarding marriage and the 'Sapinda' of the 
mother?
One cannot marry a girl who is a Sapinda of the mother.
18448
Under the rules of marriage, a person cannot marry someone who 
shares the same _____ or _____.
Gotra; Pravara.
18449
In which 7th-century text by Kumarila Bhatta was the concept of 
'Samuha Lekhya' (family records) first mentioned?
Tantra Varttika.
18450
Which historical event led to the formalisation of the Panji records 
in 1326 AD?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya unknowingly marrying his first cousin's 
daughter.
18451
Who was the King of Mithila who initiated the Panji Prabandh 
system?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
18452
In what year of the Shaka era was the Panji system established?
Shaka 1248 (1326 AD).
18453
Who was the first person appointed to manage the Panji records for 
Maithili Brahmins?
Gunaakara Jha.
18454
What is the term for the professional genealogist appointed by royal 
decree to verify marriage eligibility?
Panjikar.
18455
In the Panji system, what is a 'Beeji Purush'?
The earliest known ancestor from whom a specific lineage or 'Mool' 
originates.
18456
The Gotras Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, Savarna, Parashara, 
Bharadwaja and Katyayana are known as the _____ Gotras.
Seven Primary (or most populous).
18457
Which branch of the Samavedic marriage ceremony includes 
looking at the star Arundhati after sunset?
Uttara Vivaha.
18458
Term: Mool
Definition: The original ancestral village that serves as a unique 
identifier for a family branch.
18459
What does the 'Dushana Panji' record?
The historical flaws or violations of social/caste norms within a 
lineage to prevent their recurrence.
18460
How many specific ancestral relations, known as 'Chhadhth', are 
checked to determine marriage eligibility?
Sixteen.
18461
The rule 'Matritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami' refers to avoiding 
_____ generations on the mother's side and _____ on the father's 
side.
Five; Seven.
18462
Who is the 'Prathama Chhadhth' in the verification of a girl's 
lineage?
The girl's great-great-grandfather (Vriddha Prapitamaha).
18463
Which Maharaja of Darbhanga ordered the creation of 'Sakha 
Panjis' during the 18th century?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
18464
What does the 'Laukiki' refer to in the context of Shrotriya 
Brahmins?
The specific social status or sub-category within the Shrotriya Panji.
18465
How many 'Chauyala' (tiers) are the Shrotriya Panjis divided into?
Eight.
18466
The records for the 'Pachhwari Paith' (lower category) Brahmins 
include how many Panjis?
